mobile.title: The NovoTak mobile app provides a simplified Teams interface with essential functionality. Unlike the web version, mobile has limited settings and management capabilities, focusing on core team operations and profile management.

dashboardInterface.title: Mobile Teams Dashboard Interface:
dashboardInterface.description: The mobile teams dashboard provides a clean, simplified interface optimized for mobile devices. Here's what you'll see:
features: • **Search Bar:** Quick search functionality at the top to find team members
• **Team Member Cards:** Clean card layout showing profile pictures, names, and key information
• **Status Indicators:** Visual status indicators for each team member (Active, Inactive, External)
• **Contact Information:** Email addresses and phone numbers displayed clearly
• **Department Labels:** Department tags for easy identification
• **Designation Display:** Job titles shown for each team member
• **Touch-Optimized:** Large touch targets for easy mobile navigation

filterInterface.title: Mobile Team Filter Interface:
filterInterface.description: When users tap the filter icon in the mobile Teams interface, they can filter team members by department. Here's the filtering interface:
features: • **Department Filters:** Filter by IT, Procurement, Production, Sales, Finance, HR
• **Modal Interface:** Clean modal popup with department options
• **Touch-Friendly:** Large tap targets optimized for mobile devices
• **Smooth Animation:** Modal slides up from bottom with smooth transitions
• **Background Overlay:** Dimmed background focuses attention on filter options
• **Easy Dismissal:** Tap outside or use close button to dismiss filter

organizationTree.title: Mobile Organization Tree Structure:
organizationTree.description: The mobile organization tree provides a level-based hierarchical view of your team structure, different from the web version. Here's how it works:
organizationTree.keyDifferences: **Key Differences from Web:** Mobile organization tree uses level-based hierarchy instead of traditional tree view, department filtering buttons for quick navigation, modal-based subordinate viewing, and simplified card-based employee display optimized for touch interaction.

step1.title: Step 1: Navigate to Teams Module
instructions: Click on "Team" in the left sidebar navigation menu
Verify you are in the Teams section by checking the breadcrumb: Dashboard → Teams → List
The Teams interface will display existing team members in either Grid view or List view
step1.listViewCaption: Teams List View - Team members displayed in detailed table format with Add Member button

step2.title: Step 2: Initiate Add Member Process
instructions: Look for a blank field/empty row directly in the list view
This blank row appears at the bottom of existing team members or as the first row if no members exist
Click on any field in the blank row to start adding team member information
The form fields will become active and ready for data entry

userFormDetails.title: User Form Details:
userFormDetails.formLayout: Form Layout: Inline table format with editable fields

step2.addUserCaption: Add New User Form - Inline table format with editable fields for team member creation

step3.title: Step 3: Form Field Overview
step3.description: The user form contains several fields for entering team member information. Here's a detailed breakdown of each field:

formFields.title: Form Fields (from left to right):
formFields.id: ID: Employee ID field (can be auto-generated or manual)
formFields.name: Name: Full name input field (required)
formFields.username: Username: Email address field (required, must be unique)

requiredFields.title: Required Fields:
requiredFields.name: Name field must be completed
requiredFields.username: Username (email) must be unique and in correct format

optionalFields.title: Optional Fields:
optionalFields.designation: Designation can be left unselected or chosen from dropdown
optionalFields.department: Department can be left unselected or chosen from dropdown
optionalFields.note: Users can be added without department/designation assignment

step4.title: Step 4: Complete Form Fields
step4.addUserFormCaption: Add New User Form - Table format with all required fields for creating team members

nameField.title: 1. Name Field:
nameField.clickName: Click in the "Name" field and enter the full name of the team member
nameField.required: This is a required field and cannot be left empty

usernameField.title: 2. Username Field:
usernameField.enterEmail: Enter the email address that will serve as the username
usernameField.validFormat: Must be a valid email format (e.g., user@company.com)
usernameField.unique: Must be unique - cannot duplicate existing usernames

designationDropdown.title: 3. Designation Dropdown (Optional):
designationDropdown.clickDropdown: Click the dropdown arrow to see available job titles
designationDropdown.defaultOptions: Default options: Technician, Employee, Manager, Admin, Super Admin
designationDropdown.canBeLeft: Can be left unselected if role is not yet determined
designationDropdown.permissions: This determines the user's role-based permissions when selected

departmentDropdown.title: 4. Department Dropdown (Optional):
departmentDropdown.clickView: Click the dropdown to view available departments
departmentDropdown.defaultDepts: Default options: IT, Sales, Admin, Finance, HR, Production, Procurement
departmentDropdown.canBeLeftUnselected: Can be left unselected if department assignment is pending
departmentDropdown.assigns: This assigns the user to an organizational department when selected

branchSelection.title: Branch Selection (Department-Dependent):
branchSelection.branchAvailability: Branch Availability: Branch options appear only if the selected department has branches
branchSelection.dynamicDisplay: Dynamic Display: Branch dropdown becomes available after selecting a department with branches
branchSelection.noBranches: No Branches: If department has no branches, branch selection will not be available
branchSelection.departmentSpecific: Department-Specific: Each department may have different branch locations
branchSelection.optionalAssignment: Optional Assignment: Branch selection is optional even when available

detailsIcon.title: 5. Details Icon:

detailsIcon.configure: Configure additional user information and role assignments

userRoleAssignments.individualSelection: Individual Selection: Check specific roles as needed

roleConfigurationTips.title: Role Configuration Tips:

roleConfigurationTips.internalDefault: Internal Default: Internal users have their own permissions based on their assigned roles and departments

statusConfiguration.userControl: User Control: Users can change their own status through Team Settings (if they have permission)
statusConfiguration.permissionBased: Permission-Based: Status modification requires appropriate user permissions
statusConfiguration.teamSettingsAccess: Team Settings Access: Status changes are managed through the Team Settings interface

accountActivationStatus.nonActiveTag: Non-Active Tag: Shows when user has not yet activated their account

roleManagementOptions.title: Role Management Options:

helpNotice.title: Need Help with Permission Management?
helpNotice.description: If you're unsure how to edit permissions or use the unassign all feature, detailed step-by-step instructions are available in the "Managing Role Permissions" section below. This section includes screenshots and complete guidance for editing permissions, unassigning all permissions, and understanding the permission management interface.

roleAvailability.title: Role Availability:

roleAvailability.availableForSelection: • Available for selection when configuring user permissions
roleAvailability.canBeAssigned: • Can be assigned individually or in combination with other roles
roleAvailability.integrateSeamlessly: • Custom roles integrate seamlessly with existing role management
roleAvailability.appearInInterfaces: • Appear in all role selection interfaces throughout the application

customRoleAvailability.title: Step 4: Custom Role Availability
customRoleAvailability.imageCaption: Add New Role Dialog - Simple interface for creating custom user roles

bestPractices.title: Best Practices for Custom Roles:

bestPractices.purposeBased: • Purpose-Based: Create roles based on specific job functions or departments
bestPractices.permissionPlanning: • Permission Planning: Plan the permissions needed before creating the role
bestPractices.avoidDuplication: • Avoid Duplication: Check existing roles before creating new ones
bestPractices.futureAssignment: • Future Assignment: Once created, custom roles can be assigned to users during the add member process

managingRolePermissions.title: Managing Role Permissions:
managingRolePermissions.description: Each role in the system has specific permissions assigned to it. You can edit these permissions, delete roles, and manage permission assignments through the Roles and Permissions interface.

rolePermissionInterface.title: Role Permission Management Interface:
rolePermissionInterface.roleSelection: • Role Selection: Click on any role tab (Project Admin, Client Admin, Vendor Admin, etc.) to view its permissions
rolePermissionInterface.permissionCategories: • Permission Categories: Permissions are organized by modules (Accounting, Task, etc.)
rolePermissionInterface.visualIndicators: • Visual Indicators: Green checkmarks show assigned permissions, empty checkboxes show unassigned permissions
rolePermissionInterface.managementButtons: • Management Buttons: Each role has "Edit Permissions" and "Unassign All" buttons

editRolePermissions.permissionCategories: • Permission Categories: Permissions are grouped by module (Task, Accounting, etc.)

managingRolePermissions.editPermissionsImageCaption: Edit Permissions Dialog - Manage individual permissions for each role with search and select all options

unassignAllPermissions.title: Unassign All Permissions:

managingRolePermissions.unassignImageCaption: Unassign All Confirmation - Warning dialog before removing all permissions from a role

permissionRequirements.title: Permission Requirements:
permissionRequirements.importantNote: Important: To manage role permissions, you must have the appropriate permissions yourself. Only users with General Settings or General User Settings permissions can access and modify role permissions. If you don't have these permissions, the Edit Permissions and Unassign All buttons will not be available.

rolesPermissionsTabInterface.title: Roles and Permissions Tab Interface:
rolesPermissionsTabInterface.description: The "Roles and Permissions" tab (separate from the "Roles" tab) provides a comprehensive permissions table that lists all permissions and shows which roles have each permission assigned.
rolesPermissionsTabInterface.imageCaption: Roles and Permissions Table - Comprehensive view of all permissions with edit and management capabilities
rolesPermissionsTabInterface.roleAssignmentAccess: Role Assignment Access: If you have the appropriate permissions to assign roles to team members, you can use this interface to manage role assignments. Only users with proper administrative permissions can assign or modify roles for other team members. The role assignment functionality will be available based on your permission level.

profileManagement.title: Profile Management
profileManagement.description: View and access team member profiles based on your permissions. Team members with appropriate permissions can view profiles of other team members, including personal information, task details, and performance metrics.

webProfile.title: Web Profile Interface:
webProfile.imageCaption: Web Team Member Profile - Shows comprehensive personal information, task details, and performance analytics
webProfile.description: The web profile interface displays comprehensive information about team members including:
webProfile.personalInformation: Personal Information: Name, contact details, role, and department
webProfile.taskDetails: Task Details: Task statistics with visual charts showing overdue, completed, and in-progress tasks
webProfile.performanceMetrics: Performance Metrics: Task completion rates and productivity analytics
webProfile.navigationTabs: Navigation Tabs: Profile, Tasks, and Timesheet tabs for detailed information access
webProfile.fullDashboard: Full Dashboard: Complete analytics and performance metrics
webProfile.administrativeAccess: Administrative Access: Full profile management capabilities with permissions

webProfileAccess.title: Web Profile Access:

webProfileAccess.teamMemberProfiles: Team Member Profiles: Access profiles of team members you have permission to view
webProfileAccess.personalProfile: Personal Profile: Always have access to view your own profile information
webProfileAccess.hierarchyBased: Hierarchy-Based: Profile access follows organizational hierarchy and permission structure
webProfileAccess.fullManagement: Full Management: Edit and manage profile information with appropriate permissions
webProfileAccess.webAccessControl: Web Access Control: Profile visibility is controlled by your permissions and organizational hierarchy. Team members with appropriate permissions can view and access profiles of other team members as defined by the system administrator.

mobileProfile.title: Mobile Profile Interface:
mobileProfile.imageCaption: Mobile Team Member Profile - Simplified interface with essential information only

mobileProfile.navigationTabs: Navigation Tabs: Profile and Tasks tabs only

mobileProfileAccess.teamMemberProfiles: Team Member Profiles: View other team members' profiles with permissions

permissionsTable.title: Understanding the Permissions Table:
permissionsTable.description: In the Roles and Permissions section, you'll see a comprehensive permissions table with the following columns:

tableStructure.title: Table Structure:

addingMembers.title: Mobile Add Team Member Process:
addingMembers.mobileMultipleWays: The mobile version provides multiple ways to add team members, with permission-based access through the profile menu and organization tree interface.

addMember.title: Add Team Members

mobile.title: Add Team Members on Mobile:
mobile.description: The mobile version provides multiple ways to add team members, with permission-based access through the profile menu and the organization tree interface.

profileAccess.title: Access from Profile:
steps: • Tap 'Add Employee' from the profile menu
• The Add Employee form screen will open
• You will see a clean, mobile-optimized input form interface

organizationAccess.title: Add from Organization Tree:
organizationAccess.description: Users can also add team members directly from the organization tree interface using the add icon.
organizationAccess.workflow: **Mobile Workflow:** Create Employee via Profile → Add Employee → Assign to Organization via Organization Levels → Add Icon → Select from available members → Build hierarchical structure with subordinates.

web.title: Add Team Members on Web:
web.description: The web version provides comprehensive ways to add team members with full administrative capabilities.

listView.title: Add from List View:
listView.description: New team members are added through an empty row field in the list view instead of using an Add Team button, whether for new or existing users.

organizationChart.title: Add from Organization Chart:
organizationChart.description: Users can add members to the organization tree via the add icon, which opens a dialog to select existing team members, assign departments, and set hierarchy levels.

departments.title: Department Management
departments.description: Departments are organizational units that help structure your team. Department setup and management is handled through Team Settings on the web, while mobile users can select from existing departments when needed.

platformDifferences.web: **Web Platform:** Create, edit, and manage departments through Team Settings with full administrative control.
platformDifferences.mobile: **Mobile Platform:** Select from existing departments using dropdowns in forms and assign users.

web.title: Department Setup (Web Only):
web.description: Department creation and management is handled through Team Settings on the web platform:

mobile.title: Department Selection:
mobile.description: Select departments from dropdown lists when adding or editing team members:
features: • **User Assignment:** Assign team members to departments during user creation
• **Form Integration:** Department selection is integrated into the user management workflow
• **Existing Departments:** Select from departments created via Team Settings on the web

designations.title: Designation Management
designations.description: Designations are job titles and roles that define the positions of team members within your organization. Designation setup and management is handled through Team Settings on the web, while mobile users can select from existing designations when needed.

platformDifferences.web: **Web Platform:** Create, edit, and manage designations through Team Settings with full administrative control.
platformDifferences.mobile: **Mobile Platform:** Select from existing designations using dropdowns in forms and assign users.

web.title: Designation Setup (Web Only):

mobile.title: Designation Selection:

features: • **User Assignment:** Assign designations to team members during user creation
• **Form Integration:** Designation selection is integrated into the user management workflow
• **Existing Designations:** Select from designations created via Team Settings on the web

• **Notice Creation:** Write and save team announcements on mobile
• **Permission-Based:** Feature only available to users with notice permissions

mobileLimitations: • No editing options for team member details
• No actions menu (three dots) in mobile team lists
• Mobile users can only view team information and add new members
webOnly.recommendation: **To Edit/Delete Team Members:** Use the web version with appropriate permissions to access full team management capabilities.

• **Reorganization:** Move team members between hierarchy levels
• **Assign Subordinates:** Manage reporting relationships in the organization

features: • **Personal Information:** View and edit personal details
• **Contact Information:** Manage email and phone number
• **Role Information:** View department, designation, and status
• **Performance Metrics:** View tasks, projects, and key metrics
• **Tab Navigation:** Access different sections of the profile

• **Subordinate Profiles:** View direct subordinates' profiles

• **Tasks and Schedule Tabs:** Access tasks and track time

• **Team Profiles:** View profiles of team members in the same department

userPermissions.title: Managing User Permissions from Team List:
userPermissions.description: The "Permissions" option in the actions menu allows you to view and manage individual user permissions directly from the team list.
accessingSteps: Click the three dots (⋮) in the Actions column for any team member
The User Permissions interface will open showing all available permissions
Permission Required: Must have appropriate permissions to view/modify user permissions
userPermissions.interfaceDescription: The User Permissions page displays a comprehensive table with:

tableColumns.permissionNameDesc: Name of the specific permission (e.g., "Add Account")
tableColumns.descriptionDesc: What the permission allows (e.g., "adding account in accounting")

assigningSteps.bulkAssignmentDesc: Use "Assign All" to assign all permissions to the user
assigningSteps.confirmationDialogDesc: A dialog will ask "Are you sure you want to assign all permissions to this user?"
assigningSteps.roleBasedAssignmentDesc: Permissions are typically assigned through roles, but individual permissions can be added

userPermissions.assignPermissionsCaption: Assign Permissions Dialog - Confirmation before assigning all permissions to a user
userPermissions.bestPracticesTitle: Best Practices for User Permission Management:

bestPractices.roleBasedApproachDesc: Prefer assigning roles rather than individual permissions for easier management
bestPractices.leastPrivilegeDesc: Only assign permissions that users actually need for their job functions

bestPractices.documentationDesc: Keep track of why specific permissions were assigned to individual users

processSteps.1: Find the team member you want to remove from the hierarchy
processSteps.2: Click the red delete icon next to their name
processSteps.3: A confirmation dialog will appear asking "Are you sure you want to delete this employee from hierarchy?"
processSteps.4: Click "Delete" to confirm removal from the organizational tree
processSteps.5: The member will be moved back to the General Members List and can be reassigned later

userTypes.title: How to Assign User Types & Roles
userTypes.description: Learn how to assign user roles and types when adding team members, including default roles and creating custom roles through Team Settings.
userTypes.mobileRolesDescription: When adding employees through the mobile app, you can assign user roles using toggle switches for each available role type.
userTypes.mobileAddEmployeeAlt: Mobile Add Employee with User Roles
userTypes.mobileAddEmployeeCaption: Mobile Add Employee Form - Shows User Roles section with toggle switches for role assignment

availableRoles.superAdminDesc: Highest level access with all permissions

roleAssignmentSteps: Use toggle switches to enable/disable roles for each employee
Multiple roles can be assigned to a single employee
Roles determine system access and permissions
userTypes.webRolesTitle: Assigning User Roles from Add User Details:
userTypes.webRolesDescription: When adding new team members, you can assign user roles directly from the Add User Details dialogue box if you have the appropriate permissions.
step1Instructions: Fill in the basic user information (name, email, etc.)
Click the "Add User Details" button to open the detailed configuration form
Permission Required: Must have appropriate permissions to assign roles
userTypes.step2Description: In the User Details form, you'll find the role assignment section with multiple options:
step3Instructions: Default User Type: If no specific role is selected, users automatically default to 'Internal' status with their own permissions
Individual Selection: Check specific roles as needed for the user
Select All: Use the "Select All" checkbox to assign all available roles at once
Multiple Roles: Users can have multiple roles assigned simultaneously
Role-Based Access: Each role determines access permissions across different modules
User Type Consideration: Internal users can have multiple admin roles, external users typically have limited assignments
Permission Inheritance: Internal users inherit permissions from their user profile settings and department assignments
userTypes.userDetailsFormCaption: User Details Form - Role assignment section with default roles and selection options
userTypes.customRolesDescription: In addition to the default system roles, you can create custom roles to meet your organization's specific needs.
customRolesFeatures: Custom Role Creation: Create roles tailored to your organization's requirements
Team Settings Access: Custom roles are created through Team Settings
Permission Required: Must have appropriate permissions to create new roles
Role Assignment: Once created, custom roles become available in the user assignment process
userTypes.customRolesReference: For detailed instructions on creating custom roles, please refer to the "Roles & Permissions" section in this guide, which covers the complete process of accessing Team Settings and managing custom roles.

overview.title: Tasks Module Overview
overview.introduction: Welcome to the NovoTak Tasks module! This comprehensive guide will help you master task management across both web and mobile platforms, from basic operations to advanced workflow features.
overview.welcomeMessage: Welcome to the NovoTak Tasks module! This comprehensive guide will help you master task management, from basic operations to advanced workflow features.

web.description: The web version provides full access to all task management features including advanced views, detailed filtering, bulk operations, and comprehensive reporting tools.

crossModuleIntegration.title: Cross-Module Task Integration
crossModuleIntegration.description: Tasks in NovoTak can be created from multiple modules and will automatically appear in your main Tasks list when you're assigned or part of them.
features: • Project Tasks: Tasks created within Projects module appear in main Tasks list for assigned members
• Client Tasks: Client-related tasks are visible in Tasks list when you're involved
• Vendor Tasks: Vendor management tasks show up for relevant team members
• Lead Tasks: Lead follow-up tasks appear for assigned sales team members
• Unified View: All tasks from different modules consolidated in one central location
• Context Preservation: Tasks maintain their original module context and relationships

whatYouCanDo.title: What You Can Do in Tasks Management:

tabNavigation.title: Tab Navigation System
tabNavigation.description: The Tasks interface uses a tab-based navigation system to help you organize and filter your tasks efficiently. Each tab provides a different view of your tasks based on their status and your relationship to them.
features: • Created Tab: Shows all tasks that you have created, regardless of who they are assigned to
• Assigned Tab: Displays tasks that have been assigned to you by others or yourself
• Backlog Tab: Contains overdue tasks and items that need attention or are pending completion
• Overdue Toggle: Enable this toggle to filter and highlight tasks that have passed their due date
• Completed Late Toggle: Shows tasks that were completed after their original due date
• Quick Filtering: Click any tab to instantly filter your task view without losing your current interface settings
• Status Indicators: Each tab shows the count of tasks in that category for quick reference
• Permission-Based: Tab visibility and content depend on your organizational permissions and role and Task Tab settings

taskMemberPermissions.title: Task Member Permissions and Status
taskMemberPermissions.description: Task visibility and actions are controlled by team member roles and permissions set in the Teams module. Each team member has specific task-related permissions that determine what they can see and do.
features: • Visibility Control: Team members who are not assigned to a task will not see it in their task list
• Role-Based Actions: Each team member's actions depend on their roles and permissions given in the Teams module
• Assignment Rights: Task members can assign tasks to other team members who are not currently in that task
• Creator Privileges: Only the task creator has full control over the task and can make major modifications
• Individual Task Status: Each team member has their own task status that reflects their involvement and progress
• Dynamic Access: Task access and visibility change based on assignment status and team role updates

mobile.description: The mobile version offers essential task management capabilities optimized for on-the-go productivity, including task creation, status updates, and quick access to your assignments.

mobileInterface.title: Mobile Interface Overview
mobileInterface.description: The mobile tasks interface is optimized for phone screens with a streamlined design focused on essential task management functions. The interface provides quick access to your tasks with filtering and search capabilities.
features: • Task Tabs: Switch between Assigned, Created, and Backlog tasks using the tab navigation
• Search Function: Use the search bar to quickly find specific tasks by name or ID
• Filter System: Access advanced filtering options for Priority, Status, and Task Type
• Task Cards: View tasks in card format showing ID, title, source, and status
• Quick Actions: Tap "Open" button to access full task details and actions

taskFilterSystem.title: Task Filter System
taskFilterSystem.description: The mobile app includes a comprehensive filtering system accessible through the filter icon. This allows you to narrow down your task list based on multiple criteria.
features: • Priority Filters: Low, Medium, High priority options
• Status Filters: In Progress, Completed Late, Completed, and other status options
• Task Type Filters: General, Client, Vendor, Project, Mission categories
• Reset Option: Clear all filters to view all tasks
• Apply Filters: Use "Done" button to apply selected filter criteria

rolesPermissions.title: Roles and Permissions
rolesPermissions.description: Task creation and assignment permissions work exactly the same on mobile as they do on web platform:
features: • Task Creation: Users with task creation permissions can create tasks on mobile
• Task Assignment: Users with assignment permissions can assign tasks to team members
• Status Updates: Users can update status of tasks they have permissions for
• Team Access: Same team member visibility and assignment rules apply
• Permission Sync: All permissions are synchronized between web and mobile platforms

platformDifferences.title: Mobile Platform Differences
platformDifferences.description: The mobile version focuses on task viewing and basic management, while advanced configuration is handled through the web platform:
features: • Task Settings: Configured on web platform, settings apply to mobile automatically
• View Options: Mobile uses optimized card view instead of multiple view types
• Task Details: Full task information accessible by tapping "Open" on task cards
• Navigation: Bottom tab navigation for easy access to different modules

features.title: Mobile Task Management
features.description: Mobile interface provides streamlined task management with permission-based access and simplified workflows. The mobile version maintains the same permission structure as the web platform.
items: • Simplified Interface: Clean, touch-optimized interface designed for mobile productivity
• Permission-Based Access: Same hierarchical permissions as web version apply to mobile
• Limited View Options: Access to List and Kanban views, Gantt view may be restricted
• Quick Task Creation: Fast task creation with essential fields and assignment options
• Status Updates: Easy status changes with swipe gestures and quick actions
• Offline Capability: Basic task viewing and editing when offline, sync when connected

mobileSettingsLimitation.title: Task Settings Limitations on Mobile
mobileSettingsLimitation.description: Task settings and configuration options are not available in the mobile version of Novotak. All task settings, including task labels, statuses, categories, priorities, and excluded assignees, must be configured through the web application.
mobileSettingsLimitation.note: Note: Mobile users can view and work with tasks using settings configured by web users, but they cannot modify task configurations themselves.

altTexts.kanbanView: Kanban board view for tasks with status columns
altTexts.taskDetailsMobile: Mobile task details interface showing edit and delete icons

imageCaptions.taskLabelsInterface: Task labels interface showing default statuses, task categories, priorities, and excluded assignee configuration
imageCaptions.todoTabActivities: Tasks tab displaying the activity list and the option to add an activity
imageCaptions.addActivityForm: Add activity form with name, reminder, and member assignment
imageCaptions.activityManagement: Activity list with completion status and deletion options

keyFeatures.title: Key Features
keyFeatures.description: Task creation, assignment, status tracking, filtering, attachments, comments, and multiple view options including List, Kanban, and Gantt views.

interfaceViews.title: Interface Views
interfaceViews.description: NovoTak Tasks offers three distinct interface views to accommodate different work styles and project management needs.

listView.title: List View (Table Format)
listView.description: Traditional table format displaying tasks in rows with detailed columns for comprehensive task information.
features: • Detailed Columns: Task ID, Description, Members, Start/End Dates, Priority, Status, Category
• Inline Editing: Quick edit task details directly in the table
• Bulk Operations: Select multiple tasks for batch actions
• Sorting & Filtering: Sort by any column, filter by multiple criteria
• Export Options: Export task data to Excel or PDF formats
• Pagination: Handle large task lists with efficient pagination

gridView.title: Grid View (Kanban Board)
gridView.description: Visual kanban-style board with cards organized by status columns for intuitive task management.
features: • Status Columns: Tasks organized by workflow stages (Not Started, In Progress, Completed, etc.)
• Drag & Drop: Move tasks between status columns with simple drag and drop
• Task Cards: Visual cards showing key task information at a glance
• Custom Statuses: Create custom status columns to match your workflow
• Color Coding: Priority-based color coding for quick visual identification
• Swimlanes: Group tasks by project, assignee, or category

taskDetailsDrawer.title: Task Details Drawer
taskDetailsDrawer.description: Click on any task ID or task card to open a comprehensive details drawer with complete task information and management tools.
features: • Overview Tab: Complete task details, description, assignees, dates, priority, and status information
• Subtasks Tab: Create and manage subtasks with progress tracking and individual assignments
• Timeline Tab: Visual timeline showing task progress, milestones, and important dates
• Comments Tab: Team collaboration through comments, discussions, and task-related communications
• Activities Tab: Complete audit trail of all task changes, updates, and team interactions
• Quick Actions: Edit task details, change status, assign members, and update information directly from the drawer
• Cost & Duration Tracking: Monitor task costs and time duration with real-time updates
• File Attachments: Add and manage documents, images, and files related to the task

ganttView.title: Gantt View (Timeline Chart)
ganttView.description: Timeline-based view showing task schedules, dependencies, and project progress over time.
features: • Timeline Visualization: See task duration and scheduling on a timeline
• Dependencies: Visual representation of task dependencies and relationships
• Progress Tracking: Visual progress bars showing task completion status
• Resource Management: View team member workload and availability
• Milestone Markers: Important project milestones and deadlines
• Zoom Controls: Adjust timeline view from days to months for different perspectives

taskSettings.title: Task Settings & Configuration
taskSettings.description: Users with appropriate permissions (Super Admin and other authorized roles) can access comprehensive task settings to customize and manage the task system according to organizational needs.
features: • Task Labels: Create and manage custom labels for task categorization and organization
• Task Categories: Define project categories and task types for better classification
• Task Priorities: Set up priority levels (High, Medium, Low) with custom configurations
• Default Statuses: Manage task status workflow including In Progress, Completed, Overdue, On Hold, and Not Started
• Custom Status Creation: Add new task statuses to match specific workflow requirements
• Permission-Based Access: Only users with Super Admin or designated permissions can modify settings
• Bilingual Support: Configure labels and categories in both English and Arabic languages
• Edit & Delete Operations: Full CRUD operations for all task configuration elements

assignments.title: Task Assignments and Forwarding
assignments.description: Learn how to manage assigned tasks, change their status, and forward tasks to other team members with proper hierarchy permissions.

timeline.title: Mobile Task Timeline
timeline.description: The mobile task timeline provides a streamlined view of task activities and progress tracking optimized for mobile devices. View automatic tracking of all task activities, status changes, and team interactions in chronological order.

assignedTasks.title: Managing Assigned Tasks
assignedTasks.description: When tasks are assigned to you by others, you can view them in the 'Assigned' tab and manage their progress through status updates.

overview.title: Assigned Tasks Overview
overview.description: Tasks assigned to you appear in the 'Assigned' tab of your task interface. These are tasks created by others and assigned to you for completion.
features: • Assigned Tab: View all tasks assigned to you by other team members
• Task Details: Access complete task information including description, deadlines, and requirements
• Status Management: Update task status to reflect your progress
• Priority Indicators: See task priorities set by the task creator
• Due Date Tracking: Monitor deadlines and overdue indicators
• Member Information: View who assigned the task and other team members involved

statusUpdates.title: Updating Assigned Task Status
statusUpdates.description: As an assignee, you can only update the status of tasks assigned to you to communicate progress to the task creator and other team members. Status updates are the primary way assignees interact with assigned tasks.
steps: • Step 1: Navigate to the 'Assigned' tab in your tasks interface
• Step 2: Locate the task you want to update
• Step 3: Click on the current status or use the actions menu (three dots)
• Step 4: Select 'Change Status' from the available options
• Step 5: Choose the appropriate status from the status dialog
• Step 6: Confirm the status change to update the task
• Step 7: The task creator and other assigned members will be notified of the status change

availableActions.title: Available Actions for Assigned Tasks:
availableActions.limitedActions: • Limited Actions: Assignees have restricted permissions compared to task creators
availableActions.changeStatus: • Change Status: Primary action - Update task progress (Not Started, In Progress, Completed, etc.)
availableActions.viewDetails: • View Details: Access complete task information and history
availableActions.addComments: • Add Comments: Communicate with task creator and team members
availableActions.attachFiles: • Attach Files: Add relevant documents or files to the task (if permitted)
availableActions.forwardTask: • Forward Task: Forward the task to other eligible team members (hierarchy-based)
availableActions.noEditPermission: • No Edit Permission: Cannot modify task details, deadlines, or assignments - only status updates

taskForwarding.title: Task Forwarding
taskForwarding.description: Only assigned team members can forward tasks to other team members. Task forwarding follows organizational hierarchy rules and permission structures.

forwardingRules.title: Task Forwarding Rules and Permissions
forwardingRules.description: Task forwarding is restricted to assigned team members and follows specific hierarchy and permission rules to maintain proper task management flow.

whoCanForward.title: Who Can Forward Tasks:
whoCanForward.assignedMembers: • Assigned Members Only: Only team members who are currently assigned to the task can forward it
whoCanForward.hierarchyRules: • Hierarchy Rules: Tasks can be forwarded to team members at the same level or below in the organizational hierarchy
whoCanForward.permissionBased: • Permission-Based: Forwarding capabilities depend on your role and permissions within the organization
whoCanForward.noUpwardForwarding: • No Upward Forwarding: Tasks cannot be forwarded to team members higher in the organizational hierarchy
whoCanForward.sameLevel: • Same Level: Forward to colleagues at your organizational level
whoCanForward.belowLevel: • Below Level: Forward to team members who report to you or are at lower hierarchy levels

howToForward.title: How to Forward a Task
howToForward.description: Follow these steps to forward an assigned task to another eligible team member while maintaining proper task tracking and communication.
steps: • Step 1: Navigate to your 'Assigned' tasks tab
• Step 2: Locate the task you want to forward (you must be assigned to this task)
• Step 3: Click on the actions menu (three dots) next to the task
• Step 4: Select 'Forward Task' from the dropdown menu options
• Step 5: Choose the team member from the list of eligible recipients
• Step 6: Add any forwarding notes or instructions for the new assignee
• Step 7: Confirm the forwarding action to transfer the task
• Step 8: Both the original creator and new assignee will be notified of the forwarding

forwardingInterface.title: Task Forwarding Interface:
forwardingInterface.eligibleMembers: • Eligible Members List: Shows only team members you can forward to based on hierarchy
forwardingInterface.memberDetails: • Member Details: View recipient's name, role, department, and current workload
forwardingInterface.forwardingNotes: • Forwarding Notes: Add context or instructions for the new assignee
forwardingInterface.confirmationDialog: • Confirmation Dialog: Review forwarding details before confirming the action
forwardingInterface.notificationSystem: • Automatic Notifications: System notifies all relevant parties about the task forwarding

forwardingBestPractices.title: Task Forwarding Best Practices
practices: • Clear Communication: Always add forwarding notes explaining why the task is being forwarded
• Proper Handoff: Ensure the new assignee has all necessary information and context
• Hierarchy Respect: Follow organizational hierarchy when selecting forwarding recipients
• Workload Consideration: Consider the recipient's current workload before forwarding
• Timeline Awareness: Ensure forwarding doesn't compromise task deadlines
• Documentation: Keep track of forwarding history for accountability and reference

taskBacklog.title: Understanding Task Backlog
taskBacklog.description: The Task Backlog contains tasks that have been created but not yet assigned to team members. These tasks are in a pending state waiting for proper assignment and team member allocation.

whatIsBacklog.title: What is Task Backlog?
whatIsBacklog.description: Task Backlog is a collection of tasks that exist in the system but are not currently assigned to any team members. These tasks are visible in the 'Backlog' tab and require assignment before they can be actively worked on.
characteristics: • Unassigned Tasks: Tasks created without team member assignments
• Pending Assignment: Tasks waiting for proper team member allocation
• Visible in Backlog Tab: Accessible through the dedicated Backlog tab in the task interface
• Requires Action: Tasks need to be assigned to team members to move to active status
• Creator Visibility: Task creators can see and manage their backlog tasks
• Organizational Tool: Helps manage task workflow and assignment processes

backlogActions.title: Available Backlog Actions
backlogActions.description: Tasks in the backlog have specific actions available to help manage and organize unassigned work. These actions allow task creators to modify or remove tasks before assignment.
availableActions: • Edit Task: Modify task details, description, priority, and other properties before assignment
• Delete Task: Remove tasks that are no longer needed or were created in error
• Assign Members: Add team members to move the task from backlog to active status
• Update Priority: Change task priority to help with assignment decisions
• Modify Deadlines: Adjust due dates and scheduling before team assignment
• Add Details: Include additional information, attachments, or requirements

howTasksEnterBacklog.title: How Tasks Enter the Backlog
howTasksEnterBacklog.description: Tasks automatically move to the backlog when they are created without team member assignments. This ensures no tasks are lost and provides a central location for managing unassigned work.
scenarios: • Task Creation Without Assignment: When tasks are created but no team members are selected
• Incomplete Task Setup: Tasks saved with missing assignment information
• Bulk Task Creation: Multiple tasks created for later assignment
• Planning Phase: Tasks created during project planning before team allocation
• Template Tasks: Standard tasks created for repeated use and assignment

managingBacklog.title: Managing Your Task Backlog
managingBacklog.description: Effective backlog management ensures tasks are properly organized and assigned in a timely manner. Regular backlog review helps maintain project momentum and team productivity.
bestPractices: • Regular Review: Check backlog regularly to assign pending tasks
• Priority Organization: Set appropriate priorities for backlog tasks
• Clear Descriptions: Ensure task descriptions are complete before assignment
• Timely Assignment: Assign tasks promptly to maintain project flow
• Remove Obsolete Tasks: Delete tasks that are no longer relevant
• Batch Processing: Handle similar backlog tasks together for efficiency

altTexts.assignedTaskWeb: Assigned tasks web interface showing task list with status and forwarding options
altTexts.actionForward: Task actions menu with forward task option highlighted
altTexts.assigneeListWeb: Assignee list interface showing eligible team members for task forwarding
altTexts.createForwardTaskWeb: Create forward task dialog with team member selection and forwarding options
altTexts.backlogWeb: Task backlog interface showing unassigned tasks with edit and delete actions

subtasks.title: Subtasks Management
subtasks.description: Subtasks in NovoTak provide hierarchical task management with the same functionality as main tasks. Create subtasks within tasks, manage permissions, assign team members, and maintain organizational hierarchy.

overview.title: Subtasks Overview
overview.description: Subtasks are complete tasks created within parent tasks, maintaining the same functionality and permissions structure as main tasks. They support nested hierarchies, individual assignments, and forwarding capabilities.
keyFeatures: • Hierarchical Structure: Create subtasks within tasks and further subtasks within subtasks
• Full Task Functionality: Each subtask has the same features as main tasks including assignments, due dates, priorities, and status tracking
• Permission-Based Access: Subtask visibility and actions follow the same role and permission structure as main tasks
• Individual Assignments: Assign different team members to subtasks independent of parent task assignments
• Forwarding Capabilities: Subtask assignees can forward subtasks following organizational hierarchy
• Progress Tracking: Subtask completion contributes to parent task progress and overall project tracking
• Status Management: Independent status tracking for each subtask while maintaining parent task relationships
• Count Display: Parent tasks show subtask counts in list views for quick reference

creatingSubtasks.title: How to Create Subtasks
creatingSubtasks.description: Creating subtasks follows the same process as creating main tasks but within the context of a parent task.
steps: • Step 1: Open the parent task by clicking on the task ID or task card
• Step 2: Navigate to the 'Subtasks' tab in the task details drawer
• Step 3: Click the 'Add Subtask' button or use the blank field row at the top
• Step 4: Enter subtask title, description, and other required details
• Step 5: Assign team members based on your permissions and organizational hierarchy
• Step 6: Set due dates, priorities, and status according to project requirements
• Step 7: Save the subtask to add it to the parent task's subtask list
• Step 8: Verify subtask appears in both parent task view and assignee's task lists

subtaskInterface.title: Subtask Interface Features
subtaskInterface.description: The subtask interface provides comprehensive management tools with the same functionality as main tasks.
interfaceElements: • Subtask List View: Table format showing all subtasks with columns for ID, description, members, dates, status, and actions
• Search and Filtering: Search subtasks by description, filter by status, members, or priorities
• Status Columns: Created, Assigned, and Backlog tabs for organizing subtasks by status
• Bulk Actions: Select multiple subtasks for batch operations like status updates or assignments
• Action Menu: Three-dot menu for each subtask providing edit, delete, forward, and view options
• Progress Indicators: Visual progress bars and completion percentages for subtask tracking
• Member Avatars: Display assigned team members with profile pictures for quick identification
• Priority Indicators: Color-coded priority levels (High, Medium, Low) for visual organization

subtaskPermissions.title: Subtask Permissions and Hierarchy
subtaskPermissions.description: Subtask permissions follow the same organizational hierarchy and role-based access control as main tasks, ensuring proper workflow management.
permissionStructure: • Role-Based Access: Subtask creation and management permissions are determined by user roles and organizational hierarchy
• Hierarchical Assignment: Users can assign subtasks to team members at the same level or lower levels in the organizational structure
• Permission Inheritance: Subtasks inherit permission structures from parent tasks while maintaining independent access controls
• Visibility Controls: Subtask visibility is controlled by team member roles and department permissions
• Action Permissions: Edit, delete, and forward actions are permission-based and follow organizational hierarchy rules
• Assignment Restrictions: Users cannot assign subtasks to higher-level team members unless they have specific override permissions
• Department Boundaries: Cross-department subtask assignments require appropriate inter-department permissions
• Manager Override: Managers and supervisors can access and manage subtasks within their organizational scope

subtaskForwarding.title: Subtask Forwarding and Assignment
subtaskForwarding.description: Subtask assignees can forward subtasks to other team members following the same rules and hierarchy as main task forwarding.
forwardingRules: • Assignee Rights: Subtask assignees can forward their assigned subtasks to eligible team members
• Hierarchy Compliance: Forwarding must follow organizational hierarchy - same level or downward delegation
• Permission Validation: System validates forwarding permissions before allowing subtask transfer
• Notification System: Automatic notifications sent to original assignee, new assignee, and relevant supervisors
• Audit Trail: Complete forwarding history maintained for accountability and tracking purposes
• Status Preservation: Subtask status and progress are maintained during forwarding process
• Due Date Management: Forwarding considers due dates and timeline constraints to prevent delays
• Approval Workflow: Some forwarding actions may require supervisor approval based on organizational settings

nestedSubtasks.title: Nested Subtasks (Subtasks within Subtasks)
nestedSubtasks.description: NovoTak supports creating subtasks within subtasks, allowing for detailed project breakdown and granular task management.
nestedFeatures: • Multi-Level Hierarchy: Create subtasks within subtasks for detailed project decomposition
• Independent Management: Each level of subtasks maintains independent assignments, due dates, and status tracking
• Permission Cascading: Permissions cascade down through subtask levels while maintaining access controls
• Progress Aggregation: Nested subtask completion contributes to parent subtask and main task progress
• Visual Hierarchy: Interface displays subtask levels with indentation and visual indicators
• Navigation Tools: Easy navigation between subtask levels with breadcrumb trails and parent task links
• Bulk Operations: Perform actions on entire subtask branches or individual nested items
• Reporting Integration: Nested subtasks included in progress reports and analytics dashboards

timeline.title: Task Timeline
timeline.description: The task timeline in NovoTak automatically tracks and displays all task activities, progress updates, and status changes in chronological order. This provides a complete audit trail and visual progress tracking for every task.

overview.title: Timeline Overview
overview.description: The timeline feature automatically captures and displays all task-related activities without any manual input required. Every action performed on a task is recorded with timestamps, user information, and activity details.
keyFeatures: • Automatic Tracking: All task activities are automatically recorded without manual intervention
• Chronological Display: Activities are shown in reverse chronological order (newest first)
• User Attribution: Each activity shows which team member performed the action
• Timestamp Precision: Exact date and time stamps for all activities
• Activity Types: Tracks creation, updates, status changes, assignments, and completions
• Visual Timeline: Clean, easy-to-read timeline format with activity indicators
• Complete Audit Trail: Full history of all changes and interactions
• Real-Time Updates: Timeline updates immediately when activities occur

automaticTracking.title: How Automatic Tracking Works
automaticTracking.description: The timeline system automatically captures all task activities without requiring any manual input from users. Every interaction with a task is recorded and displayed.
trackedActivities: • Task Creation: Records when task was created and by whom
• Status Changes: Tracks all status transitions (Not Started → In Progress → Completed)
• Task Updates: Captures any modifications to task details, descriptions, or properties
• Assignment Changes: Records when team members are assigned or reassigned
• Due Date Modifications: Tracks changes to deadlines and scheduling
• Priority Updates: Records priority level changes (High, Medium, Low)
• Attachment Activities: Logs when files are added or removed
• Comment Additions: Tracks when comments or notes are added to tasks

timelineInterface.title: Timeline Interface Features
timelineInterface.description: The timeline interface provides a clear, chronological view of all task activities with detailed information for each event.
interfaceElements: • Activity Cards: Each activity is displayed in a card format with user avatar and details
• User Identification: Profile pictures and names show who performed each action
• Activity Descriptions: Clear, descriptive text explaining what action was taken
• Timestamp Display: Precise date and time information for each activity
• Activity Icons: Visual indicators for different types of activities
• Chronological Order: Most recent activities appear at the top
• Scrollable History: Full timeline history accessible through scrolling
• Activity Filtering: Option to filter timeline by activity type or date range

progressTracking.title: Progress Tracking Through Timeline
progressTracking.description: The timeline provides visual progress tracking by showing the sequence of activities and status changes, making it easy to understand task progression and identify bottlenecks.
progressFeatures: • Status Progression: Visual representation of how tasks move through different stages
• Time Analysis: See how long tasks spend in each status or phase
• Activity Frequency: Identify periods of high activity or stagnation
• Team Engagement: Track which team members are actively working on tasks
• Milestone Tracking: Important events and achievements are highlighted
• Bottleneck Identification: Easily spot where tasks get delayed or stuck
• Performance Insights: Understand team productivity and task completion patterns
• Historical Comparison: Compare current progress with past task performance

timelineBenefits.title: Benefits of Automatic Timeline
timelineBenefits.description: The automatic timeline system provides numerous benefits for project management, accountability, and team collaboration without requiring any additional effort from users.
benefits: • Zero Manual Effort: Complete activity tracking without any user input required
• Complete Transparency: Full visibility into all task activities and changes
• Accountability: Clear record of who did what and when
• Progress Monitoring: Easy tracking of task advancement and completion
• Issue Resolution: Quick identification of when and where problems occurred
• Team Coordination: Better understanding of team member contributions
• Project Insights: Valuable data for improving future project planning
• Audit Compliance: Comprehensive audit trail for regulatory or quality requirements

attachments.title: Task Attachments
attachments.description: NovoTak allows you to add various types of attachments to tasks through the comments section. You can attach files, images, documents, and add text content to provide comprehensive task documentation and collaboration.

overview.title: Attachments Overview
overview.description: The attachments feature is integrated into the task comments section, allowing team members to share files, images, documents, and text content directly within task conversations. This creates a centralized location for all task-related materials.
keyFeatures: • Multiple File Types: Support for images, documents, text files, and various file formats
• Integrated Comments: Attachments are added through the comments section for better context
• Text Content: Add formatted text content directly in comments
• File Preview: Preview supported file types directly in the interface
• Delete Options: Remove attached files when no longer needed
• Version Control: Multiple versions of files can be attached over time
• Team Collaboration: All team members can view and add attachments based on permissions
• Search Integration: Attached content is searchable within the task system

addingAttachments.title: How to Add Attachments
addingAttachments.description: Adding attachments to tasks is done through the comments section, making it easy to provide context and collaborate with team members.
steps: • Step 1: Open the task by clicking on the task ID or task card
• Step 2: Navigate to the 'Comments' tab in the task details drawer
• Step 3: Look for the attachment icons at the bottom of the comment input area
• Step 4: Click the paperclip icon to attach files or the image icon for images
• Step 5: Select files from your device using the file browser
• Step 6: Add optional text description or context in the comment field
• Step 7: Click 'Comment' button to post the attachment with your message
• Step 8: The attachment will appear in the comments timeline for all team members

attachmentTypes.title: Supported Attachment Types
attachmentTypes.description: NovoTak supports a wide variety of file types and content formats to meet different project and collaboration needs.
supportedTypes: • Images: JPG, PNG, GIF, BMP, SVG - for screenshots, diagrams, and visual references
• Documents: PDF, DOC, DOCX, TXT, RTF - for specifications, reports, and documentation
• Spreadsheets: XLS, XLSX, CSV - for data, calculations, and project tracking
• Presentations: PPT, PPTX - for project presentations and meeting materials
• Text Content: Direct text input with formatting options for notes and instructions
• Archive Files: ZIP, RAR - for multiple file collections and compressed content
• Code Files: Various programming file extensions for development projects
• Media Files: Audio and video files for multimedia project content

attachmentInterface.title: Attachment Interface Features
attachmentInterface.description: The attachment interface provides intuitive tools for adding, viewing, and managing files within task comments.
interfaceElements: • Attachment Icons: Paperclip icon for general files, image icon for pictures
• File Browser: Standard file selection dialog for choosing files from your device
• Drag & Drop: Drag files directly into the comment area for quick attachment
• File Preview: Thumbnail previews for images and file type icons for documents
• File Information: Display file name, size, and type for each attachment
• Delete Links: Click to remove attached files from the task
• Comment Integration: Attachments appear inline with comment text and context
• Multiple Attachments: Add multiple files in a single comment for comprehensive sharing
• Comment Management: Delete button available for each comment to remove unwanted entries
• Edit Control: Comment authors can manage their own comments and attachments

commentManagement.title: Comment and Attachment Management
commentManagement.description: Users have full control over their comments and attachments, with the ability to delete entries when needed.
managementFeatures: • Delete Comments: Each comment has a delete button for easy removal of unwanted entries
• Author Control: Comment authors can delete their own comments and attached files
• Attachment Removal: Deleting a comment also removes all associated attachments
• Permission-Based: Delete functionality respects user permissions and organizational hierarchy
• Confirmation Dialog: System prompts for confirmation before permanently deleting comments
• Audit Trail: Deletion activities are logged for accountability and tracking purposes
• Clean Interface: Remove outdated or incorrect information to maintain clean task conversations
• Team Coordination: Keep comment threads focused and relevant by removing unnecessary entries

attachmentBenefits.title: Benefits of Task Attachments
attachmentBenefits.description: Using attachments in task comments provides numerous advantages for project management and team collaboration.
benefits: • Centralized Documentation: All task-related files stored in one accessible location
• Context Preservation: Attachments linked to specific comments maintain conversation context
• Version History: Track file changes and updates over time through comment timeline
• Team Accessibility: All authorized team members can access shared files and documents
• Reduced Email: Eliminate need for separate file sharing through email attachments
• Project Continuity: Important files remain accessible even as team members change
• Audit Trail: Complete record of when files were shared and by whom
• Mobile Access: View and delete attachments from mobile devices for field work

commentsTodo.title: Todo Activities
commentsTodo.description: Todo Activities in NovoTak provide a powerful way to create, manage, and track checklist items within tasks. You can add team members, set dates, delete todos, and track completion with confirmation dialogs for better task organization.

overview.title: Todo Activities Overview
overview.description: The Todo Activities feature is located in the Activities tab of each task, allowing you to create actionable checklist items with team assignments, due dates, and completion tracking. This helps break down complex tasks into manageable action items.
keyFeatures: • Team Member Assignment: Add multiple team members to todo items based on permissions
• Date Management: Set due dates for individual todo items for better scheduling
• Permission-Based Access: Todo creation and management follow organizational hierarchy and permissions
• Completion Tracking: Visual progress indicators showing completed vs pending todos
• Confirmation Dialogs: System prompts for confirmation when marking todos as complete
• Delete Functionality: Remove unwanted or outdated todo items when needed
• Status Indicators: Clear visual status (Completed, Pending) for each todo item
• Real-Time Updates: Todo status updates immediately across all team members

addingTodos.title: How to Add Todo Activities
addingTodos.description: Creating todo activities is straightforward and allows you to break down tasks into specific actionable items with team assignments and deadlines.
steps: • Step 1: Open the task by clicking on the task ID or task card
• Step 2: Navigate to the 'Activities' tab in the task details drawer
• Step 3: Look for the 'To Do' section in the activities interface
• Step 4: Click 'Add' button or use the input field to create a new todo item
• Step 5: Enter the todo description or action item details
• Step 6: Click 'Add Members' to assign team members (permission-based)
• Step 7: Set due dates for the todo item if required
• Step 8: Add a reminder notification for the todo activity
• Step 9: Save the todo item to add it to the activities list

reminderSystem.title: Todo Reminders and Notifications
reminderSystem.description: When adding todo activities, you can set up reminder notifications to ensure important tasks are not forgotten. The system provides automated reminders and completion tracking.
reminderFeatures: • Add Reminder Option: When creating a todo, you can add a reminder notification
• Notification Timing: Set when you want to be reminded about the todo activity
• Team Notifications: Assigned team members receive reminder notifications
• Completion Tracking: Once reminded, you can tick the todo as completed
• Checkbox Interface: Simple tick/checkbox to mark todos as done
• Confirmation Dialog: System asks 'Are you sure you want to complete this activity?'
• Status Updates: Completed todos show green status with completion timestamp
• Progress Indicators: Visual progress showing completed vs pending todos

teamMemberManagement.title: Managing Team Members in Todos
teamMemberManagement.description: Todo activities support team member assignments based on organizational permissions, allowing you to delegate specific action items to appropriate team members.
memberFeatures: • Add Members Button: Click 'Add Members' to assign team members to todo items
• Permission-Based Assignment: Can only assign members based on your organizational hierarchy and permissions
• Multiple Assignments: Add multiple team members to a single todo item for collaborative work
• Member Profiles: Assigned members appear with profile pictures and names for easy identification
• Notification System: Assigned members receive notifications about their todo assignments
• Hierarchy Respect: Assignment follows the same rules as task assignments (same level or below)
• Remove Members: Ability to remove team members from todo assignments when needed
• Status Tracking: See which team members have completed their assigned todo items

completionTracking.title: Todo Completion and Tracking
completionTracking.description: After receiving reminders for your todo activities, you can easily mark them as completed. The system provides comprehensive tracking with visual indicators, confirmation dialogs, and progress monitoring.
trackingFeatures: • Checkbox Interface: Simple checkbox to mark todos as complete or pending
• Confirmation Dialog: System shows 'Are you sure you want to complete this activity?' prompt
• Complete/Cancel Options: Choose to confirm completion or cancel the action
• Progress Indicators: Visual display showing '1 of 1 Completed' or similar progress status
• Status Colors: Green for completed, other colors for pending status
• Timestamp Recording: System records completion date and time (e.g., '13-09-2025 12:00 AM')
• User Attribution: Shows which team member completed each todo item
• Real-Time Updates: Completion status updates immediately for all team members

todoManagement.title: Todo Management and Deletion
todoManagement.description: Users can manage their todo items by editing, deleting, and organizing them according to project needs and changing requirements.
managementFeatures: • Delete Functionality: Remove unwanted or outdated todo items from the list
• Edit Capabilities: Modify todo descriptions, assignments, and due dates as needed
• Permission Controls: Todo management respects user permissions and organizational hierarchy
• Bulk Operations: Manage multiple todo items simultaneously when needed
• Archive Options: Keep completed todos for reference or remove them to clean up the interface
• Date Management: Update due dates and scheduling for todo items
• Priority Organization: Reorder todos based on priority and importance
• Clean Interface: Remove completed or unnecessary todos to maintain focus on active items

taskSettings.title: Task Settings
taskSettings.description: Task Settings in NovoTak allow administrators and authorized users to configure various aspects of task management including task sources, priorities, statuses, and system preferences to customize the task workflow according to organizational needs.

overview.title: Task Settings Overview
overview.description: The Task Settings section provides comprehensive configuration options for customizing how tasks are created, categorized, and managed within your organization. Access to these settings is permission-based and typically available to administrators and team leads.
keyFeatures: Task Labels: Configure default task statuses with bilingual support and color coding
Task Categories: Set up custom task categories for better organization and filtering
Task Priorities: Define priority levels (Low, Medium, High) with edit and delete options
Exempted from Assigning Tasks: Add employees who cannot assign tasks to others
Create AI Permission: Grant employees access to create AI-powered task permissions
Permission-Based Access: Task settings require specific permissions to access and modify
Bilingual Interface: All settings support both English and Arabic languages
Real-Time Updates: Changes to settings are applied immediately across the system

taskLabelsStatuses.title: How to Add Task Labels and Default Statuses
taskLabelsStatuses.description: Learn how to configure and add default task statuses that appear throughout the system. These statuses help track task progress and provide visual indicators for task states.
addingSteps: Step 1: Navigate to Tasks module and click on Task Labels tab
Step 2: Review existing default statuses (In Progress, Completed Late, Completed, Submitted, Overdue, On Hold, Not Started)
Step 3: Click the green 'Add' button to create a new status
Step 4: Enter the new status name in English in the 'New Status' field
Step 5: Enter the Arabic translation in the 'Arabic New Status' field
Step 6: Click 'Add' button to save the new status to the system
Step 7: The new status will appear in the list with edit and delete options
Step 8: Use edit icon to modify existing status names or delete unwanted statuses
statusFeatures: Default Statuses: In Progress, Completed Late, Completed, Submitted, Overdue, On Hold, Not Started
Bilingual Support: Each status is displayed in both English and Arabic
Status Management: Edit existing statuses or add new custom ones using the Add button
Color Coding: Each status has associated colors for visual identification
Edit Function: Click the edit icon to modify status names and properties
Arabic Translation: Automatic Arabic translation support for new statuses
System Integration: Statuses are used across all related task modules
Visual Indicators: Status colors help users quickly identify task statuses

taskCategories.title: How to Create Task Categories
taskCategories.description: Learn step-by-step how to create and add new task categories to organize tasks by type, department, or any custom classification system that fits your organizational needs.
categoryFeatures: Category Creation: Add new task categories using the interface
Category Organization: Organize tasks by department, project type, or custom classifications
Filtering Support: Categories enable advanced filtering in task lists
Permission Control: Access to category management requires appropriate permissions
Integration Ready: Categories work with reporting and analytics features
Custom Classification: Create categories that fit your workflow requirements
Bulk Organization: Assign categories to multiple tasks for better organization
Search Enhancement: Categories improve task search and discovery
creationSteps: Step 1: Navigate to Tasks module from the main dashboard
Step 2: Click on the 'Task Categories' tab in the task settings interface
Step 3: Review any existing categories that may already be configured
Step 4: Click the 'Add' button or plus icon to create a new category
Step 5: Enter the category name in English (e.g., 'Development', 'Marketing', 'Support')
Step 6: Enter the Arabic translation for the category name
Step 7: Click 'Save' or 'Add' button to create the new category
Step 8: The new category will appear in the categories list and be available for task assignment
creationTips: Category Naming: Use descriptive names that clearly identify the type of work or department
Organizational Structure: Align categories with your company's departments or project types
Filtering Benefits: Well-named categories enable powerful filtering and search capabilities
Reporting Integration: Categories become valuable for generating departmental or type-based reports

taskPriorities.title: How to Create Task Priorities
taskPriorities.description: Learn step-by-step how to create and add new task priority levels to help teams focus on the most important work. Follow these instructions to add custom priorities beyond the default ones.
creationSteps: Step 1: Navigate to Tasks module from the main dashboard
Step 2: Click on the 'Task Priorities' tab in the task settings interface
Step 3: Review existing default priorities (Low, Medium, High)
Step 4: Click the green 'Add' button at the bottom right to create a new priority
Step 5: Enter the new priority name in English in the 'New Priority' input field
Step 6: Enter the Arabic translation in the 'Arabic New Priority' input field
Step 7: Click the 'Add' button to save the new priority to the system
Step 8: The new priority will appear in the priority list with edit and delete options available
creationTips: Priority Naming: Use clear names like 'Urgent', 'Critical', 'Routine' that reflect actual importance levels
Bilingual Consistency: Ensure Arabic translations accurately reflect the English priority meaning
Visual Styling: Each new priority automatically gets distinct visual styling for identification
System Integration: New priorities immediately become available in all task creation and editing interfaces
priorityFeatures: Default Priorities: Priority levels Low, Medium, and High are available by default
Bilingual Labels: Priority names are displayed in both English and Arabic
Priority Management: Edit existing priorities or add new custom priority levels
Visual Identification: Each priority level has a distinct visual design
Edit and Delete: Use the edit and delete icons to modify or remove priorities
Custom Priorities: Add new priority levels using the Add button
Arabic Support: Enter Arabic names for new priorities in the Arabic input field
System Integration: Priorities are used throughout task creation and management

exemptedAssignees.title: How to Add Exempted Assignees
exemptedAssignees.description: Learn step-by-step how to add employees to the exempted assignees list. These employees will be able to receive tasks but cannot assign tasks to others, helping control task delegation permissions.
addingSteps: Step 1: Navigate to Tasks module from the main dashboard
Step 2: Click on the 'Exempted from assigning tasks' tab in the task settings interface
Step 3: Review the current list of exempted employees (if any)
Step 4: Click the green 'Add Employees' button to open the employee selection dialog
Step 5: Browse through the list of available employees in your organization
Step 6: Select the employees you want to exempt from assigning tasks to others
Step 7: Click 'Add' or 'Confirm' to add the selected employees to the exempted list
Step 8: The selected employees will appear in the exempted assignees list with their profile information
exemptionEffects: Task Reception: Exempted employees can still receive and work on tasks assigned to them
Assignment Restriction: They cannot create new task assignments or delegate tasks to others
Permission Control: The system prevents them from accessing task assignment features
Organizational Hierarchy: Helps maintain proper delegation structure within teams
exemptedFeatures: Employee Selection: Add employees who are not allowed to assign tasks to others
Assignment Restrictions: Exempted employees can receive tasks but cannot delegate them
Permission Control: Prevent certain users from creating task assignments
Add Employees: Use the Add Employees button to select team members
Employee Management: View and manage the list of exempted assignees
Organizational Control: Maintain proper hierarchy for task delegation
Role-Based Restrictions: Ensure alignment with organizational roles and responsibilities
Access Management: Control who can modify the list of exempted assignees

createAIPermission.title: How to Add Create AI Permission
createAIPermission.description: Learn step-by-step how to grant specific employees access to create AI-powered task permissions. This advanced feature allows authorized users to leverage artificial intelligence for task management.
aiPermissionFeatures: AI Access Control: Add employees who can create AI-powered task permissions
Advanced Permissions: Enable AI-assisted task management for selected users
Employee Selection: Choose specific team members to access AI permissions
Permission Management: Control who can grant AI permissions to others
AI Integration: Link task management with AI features
Authorized Users Only: Restrict AI features to qualified personnel
Productivity Enhancement: AI permissions enable advanced task automation
Security Control: Maintain oversight of access to AI features within the organization
addingSteps: Step 1: Navigate to Tasks module from the main dashboard
Step 2: Click on the 'Create AI Permission' tab in the task settings interface
Step 3: Review the current list of employees with AI permissions (if any)
Step 4: Click the 'Add' button or plus icon to grant AI permissions to new employees
Step 5: Browse through the list of available employees in your organization
Step 6: Select the qualified employees who should have access to AI-powered features
Step 7: Click 'Add' or 'Confirm' to grant AI permissions to the selected employees
Step 8: The selected employees will appear in the AI permissions list and can now access AI features
permissionBenefits: AI-Powered Task Creation: Authorized employees can use AI to generate and optimize task descriptions
Advanced Automation: Access to AI-assisted task assignment and scheduling features
Enhanced Productivity: AI tools help create more efficient and detailed task management
Controlled Access: Only qualified personnel have access to advanced AI capabilities

accessPermissions.title: Task Settings Access Permissions
accessPermissions.description: Access to task settings requires specific permissions. Only authorized users can view and modify these configuration options.
permissionFeatures: Permission Required: Task settings access is restricted to authorized users only
Administrative Control: Typically available to administrators and team leads
Role-Based Access: Access depends on user roles and assigned permissions
Configuration Security: Prevents unauthorized changes to system settings
Audit Trail: Track who accesses and modifies task settings
Organizational Hierarchy: Respect organizational structure for settings access
Permission Levels: Different permission levels for viewing vs modifying settings
