Access Control: Maintain system integrity through proper permission management

taskManagement.title: Task Management Operations
taskManagement.description: Learn how to create, edit, and delete tasks with step-by-step instructions for efficient task management.

addTask.title: How to Add a New Task
addTask.description: Create new tasks with detailed information, assignments, and scheduling to organize your work effectively.
steps: • Step 1: Navigate to Tasks module from the main dashboard or sidebar menu
• Step 2: Use the blank field row at the top of the task list to input new task information
• Step 3: Enter the task description in the required Description field (Task ID and S.No. are auto-generated)
• Step 4: Use text input or voice recording feature to add detailed task description
• Step 5: Assign team members and set start/end dates in the respective columns
• Step 6: Select task type, status, and priority from the dropdown menus
• Step 7: Click on the Source field to open the 'Add task details' dialog for additional configuration
• Step 8: In the dialog, select Task Type from options (General, Project, Mission, Client, Vendor)
• Step 9: Choose Task Category from available options (customizable in task settings)
• Step 10: Click 'Add' to confirm task details, then 'Save' to create the task

formFields.title: Step 4: Complete Form Fields
formFields.description: Fill in the task information directly in the blank field row at the top of the task list. Each field serves a specific purpose in task organization and management.
fields: 1. S.No. (Serial Number):
• Automatically generated sequential number for task identification
• System assigns this number when task is saved
• Cannot be manually edited or changed
• Used for quick task reference and sorting
2. Task ID Field:
• System-generated unique identifier appears after saving
• Automatically created based on task sequence
• Cannot be manually entered or modified
• Provides permanent task identification across the system
3. Source Field :
• Click to open 'Add task details' dialog
• Must select Task Type from dropdown options
• Available options: General, Project, Mission, Client, Vendor
• This categorizes the task origin and determines workflow
4. Description Field (Required):
• Click in the description field to enter task details
• Can type directly or use voice recording feature (microphone icon)
• Must provide clear, detailed explanation of task requirements
• This is a mandatory field - task cannot be saved without description
• Voice recording converts speech to text automatically and creates transcripts
• System generates transcripts in multiple languages (English, Arabic) for voice recordings
• Transcripts show original description and translations for better understanding
Important Note for New Users:
• Tasks created with only description (no team members assigned) will not appear in the main task list
• These tasks automatically go to the Backlog until team members are assigned
• To see tasks in the main list, always assign at least one team member during creation
• This helps organize tasks and ensures proper task assignment workflow
5. Members Assignment:
• Assign to me: Toggle this option to automatically assign the task to yourself
• Confidential task: Toggle this option to make the task confidential - only visible to assigned members
• Search Members: Use the search bar to find specific team members
• Select Members: Check the boxes next to team member names to assign them
• Multiple Assignment: You can assign the task to multiple team members
• Member Details: Each member shows ID, designation, department, and current status
• Status Indicator: Shows member availability status (Available/Not Available)
• Department Organization: Members are organized by department for easy selection
• Assignment Hierarchy: Tasks should be assigned to members at the same level or to members below you in the organizational hierarchy
6. Sub (Subtask Indicator):
• Shows if task has related subtasks
• Automatically populated when subtasks are created
• Cannot be manually edited in main task creation
• Managed through subtask creation process
7. Start Date Field:
• Click to open date picker calendar
• Select when task work should begin
• Can be set to current date or future date
• Optional field - can be left blank initially
• Helps in task scheduling and timeline planning
8. End Date Field:
• Click to open date picker calendar
• Set target completion deadline
• Should be after start date if both are set
• Optional field but recommended for time management
• Used for deadline tracking and overdue notifications
9. Type Dropdown:
• Click dropdown arrow to see available task types
• Source-Based Categorization: Type options automatically categorize tasks based on Source selection
• Project Tasks: When Source is 'Project', task will be categorized as project-related
• General Tasks: When Source is 'General', task will be categorized as general work
• Client Tasks: When Source is 'Client', task will be categorized as client-related
• System Configuration: Available options depend on Source selection and system settings
• Administrator Control: Can be customized in task settings by administrators
• Filtering & Reporting: Helps categorize tasks for easy filtering and comprehensive reporting
• Flexible Assignment: Optional field - can be set later if needed during task creation
10. Status Dropdown:
• Click dropdown to select current task status
• Default options: Not Started, In Progress, Completed, On Hold, Overdue
• New tasks typically start with 'Not Started' status
• Status can be changed as work progresses
• Custom statuses can be added in task settings
11. Priority Dropdown:
• Click dropdown arrow to set task importance level
• Standard options: High, Medium, Low
• High priority tasks appear prominently in lists
• Affects task sorting and team member notifications
• Can be changed later based on business needs
12. Action Field:
For New Tasks:
• Save Button: Click to create the task and add to task list
• Delete Button: Clear all fields and remove the task row
• Save Requirements: Save is only enabled when required fields are completed
• Notifications: Saving triggers notifications to assigned team members
• Task Visibility: Task appears in all relevant views after saving
For Existing Tasks (Action Menu):
• Edit: Modify task details, assignments, dates, and other properties
• Change Status: Update task status (Not Started, In Progress, Completed, etc.)
• Delete: Permanently remove the task from the system
• Menu Access: Click the three dots icon on existing tasks to access the action menu

taskDetailsDialog.title: Add Task Details Dialog:
taskDetailsDialog.description: When clicking on the Source field, a dialog opens with additional task configuration options.
fields: • Task Type: Select from predefined options - General, Project, Mission, Client, Vendor
• Task Category: Choose from available categories (can be customized in task settings)
• Default Options: System provides default category options that can be modified by administrators
• Dialog Actions: 'Cancel' to close without saving, 'Add' to confirm and apply selections

editTask.title: How to Edit an Existing Task
editTask.description: Modify task details, update assignments, change priorities, and adjust schedules as project requirements evolve.
steps: • Step 1: Locate the task you want to edit in any view (List, Grid, or Gantt)
• Step 2: Click on the task ID, title, or use the actions menu (three dots)
• Step 3: Select 'Edit' from the dropdown menu or click the edit icon
• Step 4: Modify the required fields in the task editing form
• Step 5: Update assignments, dates, priority, or status as needed
• Step 6: Add or remove team members and adjust permissions
• Step 7: Save changes to update the task and notify affected team members

editableFields.title: Editable Task Fields:
fields: • Task Information: Title, description, and detailed requirements
• Assignment Changes: Add or remove team members from the task
• Schedule Updates: Modify due dates, start dates, and time estimates
• Priority Adjustments: Change task priority based on current needs
• Status Updates: Move task through workflow stages
• Category Changes: Reassign to different project categories
• Attachment Management: Add new files or remove existing attachments
• Subtask Modifications: Create, edit, or delete subtasks

statusManagement.title: Task Status Management
statusManagement.description: Efficiently manage and update task statuses to track progress and maintain workflow visibility across your team.

overview.title: Status Management Overview
overview.description: NovoTak provides flexible status management options allowing you to update task progress through multiple interfaces and maintain clear workflow visibility.
features: • Multiple Access Points: Change status from actions menu or direct status field click
• Status Dialog Interface: Comprehensive status selection with search functionality
• Real-time Updates: Instant status changes reflected across all team views
• Custom Status Options: Default statuses with ability to create custom workflow states
• Visual Indicators: Color-coded status representation for quick identification
• Team Notifications: Automatic updates to assigned team members on status changes

actionMenu.title: Method 1: Using Actions Menu
steps: • Step 1: Locate the task in your task list or board view
• Step 2: Click on the actions menu (three dots) next to the task
• Step 3: Select 'Change Status' from the dropdown menu options
• Step 4: Choose the desired status from the status dialog that opens
• Step 5: Click 'Change' to apply the new status to the task

directClick.title: Method 2: Direct Status Field Click
steps: • Step 1: Find the task with the status you want to change
• Step 2: Click directly on the current status field/badge
• Step 3: The status change dialog will open automatically
• Step 4: Select the new status from the available options
• Step 5: Confirm the change by clicking 'Change' button

statusDialog.title: Status Selection Dialog Features
statusDialog.description: The status change dialog provides comprehensive options for managing task progress states.
features: • Search Functionality: Quickly find specific statuses using the search bar
• Status Categories: Organized status options for different workflow stages
• Visual Selection: Clear checkboxes and visual indicators for current selection
• Default Statuses: Pre-configured workflow states (In Progress, Completed, etc.)
• Custom Statuses: Organization-specific status options if configured
• Cancel/Change Options: Easy confirmation or cancellation of status updates

availableStatuses.title: Available Task Statuses
defaultStatuses: • Not Started: Task has been created but work hasn't begun
• In Progress: Task is currently being worked on
• Completed Late: Task finished after the due date
• Submitted: Task completed and submitted for review
• On Hold: Task temporarily paused or waiting for dependencies
• Overdue: Task has passed its due date without completion

bestPractices.title: Status Management Best Practices
tips: • Regular Updates: Update task statuses promptly to maintain accurate project visibility
• Clear Communication: Use status changes to communicate progress to team members
• Workflow Consistency: Follow established status progression patterns within your team
• Due Date Awareness: Monitor overdue statuses and address delays proactively
• Review Processes: Use 'Submitted' status for tasks requiring approval or review
• Hold Management: Clearly document reasons when placing tasks 'On Hold'

updatingStatus.title: Updating Task Status on Mobile
updatingStatus.description: Task status can be easily updated directly from the task details screen using the status dropdown in the task header.
features: • Status Dropdown: Tap the status field in task header to open status options
• Available Statuses: In Progress, Completed Late, Completed, Submitted, Overdue, On Hold, Not Started
• Quick Selection: Tap any status to immediately update the task
• Visual Feedback: Status changes are reflected immediately in the interface

availableStatusOptions.title: Available Status Options
availableStatusOptions.description: The mobile interface provides access to all configured task statuses with clear descriptions:
statuses: • Not Started: Task has been created but work hasn't begun
• In Progress: Task is currently being worked on
• On Hold: Task is temporarily paused or waiting for dependencies
• Completed: Task has been finished successfully within deadline
• Completed Late: Task finished after the due date
• Submitted: Task has been submitted for review or approval
• Overdue: Task has passed its due date without completion

filteringByStatus.title: Filtering by Status
filteringByStatus.description: Use the mobile filter system to view tasks by specific status. This helps you focus on tasks in particular stages of completion.
features: • Filter Access: Tap the filter icon in the task list header
• Status Filter: Select specific statuses to display in the task list
• Multiple Selection: Choose multiple statuses for broader filtering
• Clear Filters: Reset filters to view all tasks regardless of status

statusManagementNotes.title: Mobile Status Management Notes
statusManagementNotes.description: Important points about status management on mobile:
features: • Status Configuration: Custom status options are created and managed through the web platform
• Real-time Sync: Status changes on mobile are immediately reflected on web and vice versa
• Permission Control: Status update permissions are managed through the web Teams module
• Automatic Updates: Some statuses like "Overdue" are automatically set by the system
• Status History: All status changes are tracked and visible in task timeline

deleteTask.title: How to Delete a Task
deleteTask.description: Remove tasks that are no longer necessary while ensuring data integrity and communication between team members.
steps: • Step 1: Search for the task you want to delete in your task list or view.
• Step 2: Click on the action menu (three dots) next to the task.
• Step 3: Select 'Delete' from the dropdown options.
• Step 4: Confirm the deletion in the confirmation box that appears.
• Step 5: Review any warnings related to task dependencies or assignments.
• Step 6: Click 'Confirm Deletion' to permanently remove the task.
• Step 7: The system will notify affected team members of the task deletion.

considerations.title: Important Considerations:
points: • Permissions Requirement: Only task creators or users with deletion permissions can delete tasks.
• Data Loss Warning: Deleted tasks cannot be restored — consider archiving them instead of deleting if you're unsure.
• Team Notifications: All assigned members will be notified about the task deletion.
• Dependency Impact: Check for any sub-tasks or tasks dependent on this task before deleting it.

deleteTask.ticketTasksNote: • Ticket Task Note: Actions (such as deletion, modification, or status changes) cannot be performed on tasks linked to tickets within projects through the task module. These actions can only be carried out through the ticket itself.

creatingTasks.title: Creating Tasks on Mobile
creatingTasks.description: The mobile task creation interface is streamlined for quick task entry with essential fields optimized for touch interaction.
features: • Description Field: Main text area with voice input option (microphone icon)
• Category Selection: Dropdown to select task category
• Task Type: Choose from General, Client, Vendor, Project, Mission
• Date Selection: Start date and Due date pickers
• Priority Levels: Low, Medium, High priority buttons
• Self Assignment: "Assign to me" checkbox for quick self-assignment
• Team Assignment: "Add Members" button to assign to team members

memberSelection.title: Team Member Selection
memberSelection.description: When assigning tasks to team members, a dedicated member selection screen appears with search functionality.
features: • Search Members: Search bar to quickly find team members
• Member List: Shows employee ID, name, role, and department
• Profile Pictures: Visual identification with member photos
• Multiple Selection: Select multiple team members for task assignment
• Done Button: Confirm member selection and return to task creation

taskDetailsEditing.title: Task Details and Editing
taskDetailsEditing.description: Task details screen provides comprehensive task information with edit and delete options accessible through the top action icons.
features: • Task Header: Shows task ID, type, and status dropdown for quick updates
• Action Icons: Delete (trash) and Edit (pencil) icons in top right
• Task Information: Cost, time, category, and other task details
• Tab Navigation: Todo, Timeline, Subtasks, Comments tabs
• Assigned Members: Shows task creator and assigned team members
• Status Updates: Dropdown to change task status directly

editingTasks.title: Editing Tasks
editingTasks.description: Tap the edit icon (pencil) in task details to modify task information. The edit interface is similar to task creation with pre-filled values.
features: • Pre-filled Fields: All current task information is loaded for editing
• Member Management: Add or remove assigned members with red minus icons
• Field Updates: Modify description, category, dates, priority as needed
• Save Changes: Green "Save" button to confirm modifications

deletingTasks.title: Deleting Tasks
deletingTasks.description: Tasks can be deleted from the task details screen using the delete icon (trash) in the top right corner.
features: • Delete Icon: Tap the trash icon in task details header
• Confirmation Dialog: "Are you sure you want to delete this task?" prompt
• Cancel Option: Blue "Cancel" button to abort deletion
• Confirm Deletion: Red "Delete" button to permanently remove task
• Permission Required: Only users with delete permissions can remove tasks

interfaceDifferences.title: Mobile Interface Differences
interfaceDifferences.description: The mobile task management interface is optimized for touch interaction with some differences from web:
features: • Simplified Fields: Fewer fields compared to web for streamlined mobile experience
• Voice Input: Microphone icon for voice-to-text description entry
• Touch Optimized: Larger buttons and touch-friendly interface elements
• Tab Navigation: Todo, Timeline, Subtasks, Comments organized in tabs
• Quick Actions: Edit and delete accessible via header icons

projectTaskCreation.title: Creating Tasks Within Projects on Mobile
projectTaskCreation.description: Mobile users can create tasks directly within projects using the same comprehensive task creation interface available in the main task management section. This feature provides full task creation capabilities with permission-based access.
projectTaskCreation.imageAlt: Mobile task creation form within project detail view
projectTaskCreation.imageCaption: Mobile task creation form with description, category, task type, project selection, dates, and priority options

howToAccess.title: How to Access Task Creation:
howToAccess.step1: Navigate to the project detail view
howToAccess.step3: Tap the "+" (plus) button to open the task creation form
howToAccess.step4: Permission required: Users must have task creation permissions

features.title: Available Task Creation Features:
features.feature1: Description field with voice input support (microphone icon)
features.feature3: Task type selection (General, Client, Vendor, Project, Mission)
features.feature4: Project assignment (automatically set to current project)
features.feature5: Start date and due date selection
features.feature7: Self-assignment option with "Assign to me" checkbox
features.feature8: Team member assignment through "Add Members" functionality

permission.title: Permission Required:
permission.description: Task creation within projects requires appropriate permissions. Users without task creation permissions will not see the "+" button in the Tasks section.

mobileSettingsLimitation.title: Mobile Task Settings Limitation
mobileSettingsLimitation.description: Task settings and configuration options are not available in the mobile version of NovoTak. All task settings, including task labels, statuses, categories, priorities, and exempted assignees, must be configured through the web application.
mobileSettingsLimitation.note: Note: Mobile users can view and work with tasks using the settings configured by web users, but cannot modify the task configuration themselves.

altTexts.taskDetailsMobile: Mobile Task Details Interface showing Edit and Delete Icons

imageCaptions.taskLabelsInterface: Task labels interface showing default statuses, task categories, priorities, and exempted assignees configuration
imageCaptions.todoTabActivities: Todo tab showing activities list and add activity option
imageCaptions.addActivityForm: Add activity form with name, reminder, and member assignment
imageCaptions.activityManagement: Activity list with completion status and delete options
imageCaptions.taskTimeline: Task timeline showing automatic tracking of all activities, status changes, and progress updates
imageCaptions.overviewDocument: Task overview document showing comprehensive task management features and interface elements
imageCaptions.documentAddForm: Document add form interface for attaching files and documents to tasks
imageCaptions.mobileBacklog: Mobile backlog interface showing unassigned tasks with basic management operations
imageCaptions.mobileTimeline: Mobile timeline interface showing task activity history and progress tracking
imageCaptions.taskComments: Task comments interface showing team collaboration and communication features
imageCaptions.taskActionsChangeStatus: Task actions menu showing status change options and workflow controls
imageCaptions.taskActionsForward: Task actions menu with forward task option highlighted for team collaboration
imageCaptions.forwardTaskInterface: Forward task interface showing team member selection and forwarding options
imageCaptions.mobileTaskAttachments: Mobile task attachments interface showing document, camera, and gallery options for adding files

overview.title: Mobile Task Details Overview
overview.description: The mobile task details interface provides comprehensive task information with expandable sections and intuitive navigation optimized for mobile devices.
features: • Task Header: Shows task ID, type, and status dropdown for quick updates
• Action Icons: Delete (trash) and Edit (pencil) icons in top right
• Task Information: Cost, time, category, and other task details
• Tab Navigation: Todo, Timeline, Subtasks, Comments tabs
• Assigned Members: Shows task creator and assigned team members

tabs.title: Task Details Tabs
tabs.description: The task details drawer is organized into multiple tabs for easy navigation and information access:

overview.title: Overview Tab
overview.description: Displays core task information: description, assignees, dates, priority, status, and progress.

documentEditing.title: Document-Style Editing Feature:
documentEditing.description: Existing task details can be edited using a rich text editor with comprehensive formatting toolbar including formatting options, lists, tables, links, and more to create detailed and professionally formatted documents.

assignments.title: Task Assignments and Forwarding
assignments.description: Assign tasks to team members and forward them for follow-up or approval

mobile.title: Mobile Task Assignments
mobile.description: On mobile, you can view your assigned tasks and update their status. Task assignment and forwarding are handled through the web platform. No forward task functionality is available on mobile.

timeline.title: Mobile Task Timeline
timeline.description: The mobile task timeline provides a streamlined view of task activities and progress tracking optimized for mobile devices. View automatic tracking of all task activities, status changes, and team interactions in chronological order.

features: • View Assigned Tasks: Access tasks assigned to you through the Assigned tab
• Update Status: Change task status through task details screen
• Task Details: Tap on task to view details without edit/delete options for assigned tasks
• No Forward Function: Task forwarding functionality is not available on mobile

backlog.title: Mobile Task Backlog
backlog.description: Mobile backlog functionality works the same as web tasks but has no members assignment functionality. You can view and manage backlog tasks with basic operations.
features: • View Backlog Tasks: Access unassigned tasks through the Backlog tab
• Task Management: Same functionality as web tasks for basic operations
• No Member Assignment: Cannot assign members to tasks from mobile interface
• Status Updates: Can update task status and basic task information

subtasks.title: Mobile Subtasks Management
subtasks.description: Subtask functionality on mobile provides the same hierarchical task management as the web version, allowing you to create, manage, and track subtasks with full task features and nested capabilities.
features: • Same as Main Tasks: Subtask functionality identical to main task management
• Task Detail Access: Click subtasks tab from main task detail screen
• Task Count Display: Shows main task reference and hierarchy
• Nested Subtasks: Create subtasks within existing subtasks for complex hierarchies
• Full Task Features: All task creation and management features available

attachments.title: Mobile Task Attachments
attachments.description: Mobile version provides full attachment functionality allowing you to add files, photos, and documents directly from your device. Access attachment options through the task details interface.
options: • Document : Upload files and documents from device storage
• Camera : Take photos directly and attach them to tasks
• Gallery:: Select photos and media from device gallery

todoActivities.title: Mobile Todo Activities
todoActivities.description: Mobile todo activities provide task-related activity and reminder management. Access the todo interface from the task detail screen by selecting the Todo tab. Create activities, assign team members, set reminders, and track completion status just like the web version.
features: • Access Method: Navigate to Todo tab within task detail interface
• Activity Creation: Generate new activities with custom names and reminder settings
• Team Collaboration: Assign team members to specific activities
• Date Management: Set reminder dates and track activity deadlines
• Activity Operations: Mark complete, delete, and manage activity status
• Save & Add More: Save current activity and continue adding more activities

mobileTaskManagement.title: Mobile Task Management
mobileTaskManagement.description: NovoTak mobile app provides streamlined task management optimized for on-the-go productivity. The mobile interface focuses on essential features with touch-friendly design and simplified workflows.

overview.title: Mobile Task Management Overview
overview.description: The mobile version offers core task management functionality with a clean, touch-optimized interface designed for mobile productivity and quick task operations.
keyFeatures: • Touch-Optimized Interface: Clean, mobile-friendly design with large touch targets and intuitive navigation
• Essential Features Only: Streamlined functionality focusing on core task operations without complex features
• Quick Actions: Fast task creation, status updates, and assignment changes with minimal taps
• Permission-Based Access: Same organizational hierarchy and permissions as web version
• Offline Capability: Basic task viewing and editing when offline, automatic sync when connected
• Push Notifications: Real-time notifications for task assignments, updates, and deadlines

mobileInterface.title: Mobile Task Interface
mobileInterface.description: The mobile task interface is designed for efficiency and ease of use on smaller screens with simplified navigation and essential controls.
interfaceElements: • Task List View: Simplified list format showing essential task information with swipe actions
• Search and Filter: Quick search bar and basic filtering options for finding tasks
• Status Tabs: Easy navigation between Created, Assigned, and Backlog tasks
• Floating Action Button: Quick access to create new tasks from any screen
• Swipe Gestures: Swipe left/right for quick actions like status change or task forwarding
• Pull to Refresh: Simple pull-down gesture to refresh task lists and sync data

mobileTaskCreation.title: Creating Tasks on Mobile
mobileTaskCreation.description: Mobile task creation is streamlined with essential fields and quick input methods optimized for touch devices.
creationSteps: • Step 1: Tap the floating '+' button or 'Create Task' option
• Step 2: Enter task title using the mobile keyboard
• Step 3: Add task description with voice-to-text support if available
• Step 4: Select team members from your contact list or organization directory
• Step 5: Set due date using the mobile date picker
• Step 6: Choose priority level (High, Medium, Low) with simple tap selection
• Step 7: Select task category from predefined options
• Step 8: Tap 'Create Task' to save and assign the task
mobileFeatures: • Voice Input: Use voice-to-text for task descriptions and notes
• Contact Integration: Select team members from device contacts
• Camera Integration: Add photos directly from camera or gallery
• Location Services: Add location context to tasks if enabled
• Quick Templates: Use predefined task templates for common activities

mobileStatusManagement.title: Mobile Status Management
mobileStatusManagement.description: Update task status quickly on mobile with swipe gestures and simplified status selection designed for touch interaction.
statusUpdateMethods: • Swipe Actions: Swipe left or right on tasks to reveal quick status change options
• Tap to Update: Tap on current status to open status selection dialog
• Bulk Updates: Select multiple tasks and update status in batch operations
• Voice Commands: Use voice commands to update task status (if supported)
• Quick Status Bar: Access common status changes from the task detail screen
mobileStatusFeatures: • Visual Status Indicators: Color-coded status badges for quick visual identification
• Progress Tracking: Simple progress bars and completion percentages
• Status History: View status change timeline in simplified mobile format
• Notification Updates: Automatic notifications when status changes are made
• Offline Status Changes: Update status offline and sync when connection is restored

mobileTaskAssignment.title: Mobile Task Assignment
mobileTaskAssignment.description: Assign tasks to team members using mobile-optimized contact selection and simplified assignment workflows.
assignmentFeatures: • Contact Integration: Select team members from device contacts or organization directory
• Recent Assignees: Quick access to frequently assigned team members
• Department Filtering: Filter team members by department or role for easier selection
• Multiple Selection: Assign tasks to multiple team members with multi-select interface
• Assignment Notifications: Automatic push notifications sent to assigned team members
• Permission Validation: Real-time validation of assignment permissions based on hierarchy
mobileAssignmentSteps: • Step 1: Open the task you want to assign or create a new task
• Step 2: Tap on the 'Assign Members' field or button
• Step 3: Browse or search for team members in the mobile-friendly list
• Step 4: Tap to select one or multiple team members
• Step 5: Review selected assignees and their roles
• Step 7: Assigned members receive push notifications about the new task

projectManagement.title: Project Management
projectManagement.subtitle: Learn how to create, manage, and organize projects effectively in NovoTak

overview.title: Overview
overview.description: Introduction to project management features and capabilities

projectDetailOverview.title: Project Detail Overview
projectDetailOverview.description: Learn about the project overview tab and its rich text documentation features

projectTasks.title: Project Tasks
projectTasks.description: Learn how to create, manage, and work with tasks within projects including editing, deleting, timesheet, activities, and subtasks

taskCategory.title: Task Category
taskCategory.description: Learn how to select and create task categories to organize and classify your tasks effectively

milestone.title: Milestone
milestone.description: Learn how to create project milestones, add tasks to milestones, and track project progress effectively

filesDocuments.title: Files & Documents
filesDocuments.description: Overview of project file and document management with folders, uploads, and organization features

createProject.title: Create Project
createProject.description: Learn how to create new projects with all necessary details

missionManagement.title: Mission Management
missionManagement.description: Learn how to create and manage missions - short-term, time-bound tasks with specific objectives

projectActions.title: Project Actions
projectActions.description: Learn how to edit, archive, and change project status

viewArchivedProjects.title: View Archived Projects
viewArchivedProjects.description: Access and manage your archived projects

projectSettings.title: Project Settings
projectSettings.description: Configure project goals, initiatives, and permissions with add, edit, and delete capabilities based on user permissions

overview.title: Project Management Overview
overview.description: NovoTak's project management system helps you organize, track, and manage projects from creation to completion. Create projects, assign team members, set deadlines, and monitor progress all in one place.
overview.introduction: The Project Management module helps you organize, track, and manage projects from start to finish. Create projects, assign team members, set milestones, track budgets, and monitor progress all in one place.

features.projectCreation: Create projects with detailed information, timelines, and team assignments
features.teamCollaboration: Assign team members and manage project roles and permissions
features.statusTracking: Track project progress with customizable status options
features.archiveSystem: Archive completed projects while preserving all data
features.departmentOrganization: Organize projects by departments and categories

projectDetails.title: Project Details View
projectDetails.description: When you click on a project, you'll see a comprehensive dashboard with all project information, task statistics, and team details. The Project Details view is your central hub for managing and monitoring project progress.

features.title: What You'll See in Project Details

info.title: Project Information
info.description: Project name, description, creator, owners, members, initiative, goal, client, milestone, and dates.

tasks.title: Task Statistics
tasks.description: Visual cards and charts showing tasks by status: In Progress, Completed, Submitted, On Hold, Not Started.

myTasks.title: My Project Tasks
myTasks.description: Personal task list and chart showing your assigned tasks within this project.

actions.title: Quick Actions
actions.description: Add creators and owners, view total task cost and duration, and access project closure options.

projectDetails.learnMore: For detailed information about all dashboard features, see the "Project Details" section in the menu.

overview.whatYouCanDo: What You Can Do in Project Management

interfaceViews.title: Interface Views
interfaceViews.description: The Project Management module offers two different view types to help you visualize and manage your projects effectively. Switch between List View and Grid View based on your preference and workflow needs.

listView.title: 1. List View
listView.description: The List View displays projects in a detailed table format, showing all key information in rows and columns. This view is ideal for quickly scanning multiple projects and comparing their details side by side.
listView.benefit1: Viewing detailed project information at a glance
listView.benefit2: Sorting and filtering projects by various criteria
listView.benefit3: Comparing multiple projects side by side
listView.benefit4: Managing large numbers of projects efficiently

gridView.title: 2. Grid View
gridView.description: The Grid View displays projects as cards in a visual grid layout. Each card shows key project information with visual indicators like progress bars and status badges. This view provides a more visual and intuitive way to browse your projects.
gridView.benefit2: Quick identification of project status and progress
gridView.benefit3: Browsing projects with a more visual approach
gridView.benefit4: Better use of screen space on larger displays

switchViews.title: How to Switch Views
switchViews.description: You can easily switch between List View and Grid View using the view toggle buttons located in the top-right corner of the Projects page. Your view preference will be saved automatically for your next visit.

navigation.title: Tabs, Navigation, Filters & Search
navigation.description: The Project Management interface provides powerful tools to help you navigate, filter, and search through your projects efficiently. Use tabs to switch between different project types, apply filters to narrow down results, and search to quickly find specific projects.

tabs.title: 1. Tabs Navigation
tabs.description: Use the tabs at the top of the page to switch between different sections:
tabs.dashboardDesc: Overview of all projects with key metrics and statistics
tabs.projectsDesc: View and manage all your projects
tabs.listDesc: Detailed list view of all projects with full information

viewToggle.title: 2. View Toggle
viewToggle.description: Switch between List View and Grid View using the toggle buttons:
viewToggle.listDesc: Click to switch to detailed table view
viewToggle.gridDesc: Click to switch to card-based grid view

search.title: 3. Search
search.description: Use the search bar to quickly find specific projects:
search.feature1: Type project name, code, or keywords in the search box
search.feature2: Results update instantly as you type
search.feature3: Search works across all project fields
search.feature4: Clear search to view all projects again

filters.title: 4. Filters
filters.description: Apply filters to narrow down your project list based on specific criteria:
filters.statusDesc: Filter by project status (Active, Completed, On Hold, etc.)
filters.departmentDesc: Show projects from specific departments only
filters.multipleDesc: Combine multiple filters for precise results
filters.clearDesc: Reset all filters to view all projects

tips.title: Pro Tips
tips.tip1: Combine search and filters for the most precise results
tips.tip2: Use the Status filter to focus on active projects
tips.tip3: Switch to Grid View for a visual overview, List View for detailed information
tips.tip4: Your view and filter preferences are saved automatically

additionalFeatures.title: Additional Features
additionalFeatures.description: The Project Management module includes additional powerful features to help you organize and control your projects:

archive.title: Archive Projects
archive.description: Move completed or inactive projects to archive without deleting them. Archived projects can be viewed separately and reactivated anytime. See the Archive section in the menu for more details.

settings.title: Project Settings
settings.description: Configure project goals, initiatives, and permissions. Manage who can view and create projects across your organization. See the Project Settings section in the menu for complete details.

mobile.title: Mobile Project Management
mobile.description: Access your projects on the go with the mobile app. View project details, update status, and collaborate with your team from anywhere.
mobile.imageAlt: Mobile project detail view showing project information and navigation
mobile.whatYouCanDo: What You Can Do on Mobile

access.title: Project Access
access.item1: View complete project list with search functionality
access.item2: Access detailed project information and status
access.item3: Navigate between Projects, Mission, and Archive tabs

collaboration.title: Team Collaboration
collaboration.item1: View project team members and owner information
collaboration.item2: Check project progress and updates in real-time

filter.title: Project Filter
filter.description: Use the mobile project filter to quickly find and organize your projects by status and other criteria.
filter.feature1: Filter projects by status (Created, Started, Closed, Cancelled, In Progress)

projectDetailOverview.introduction: The Project Overview tab is a dedicated space within each project where you can document project details, objectives, and important information using a rich text editor. This tab serves as the central documentation hub for your project.

whatIs.title: What is the Project Detail Overview Tab?
whatIs.description: The Project Detail Overview tab is a comprehensive documentation space that allows you to create detailed project descriptions, objectives, and documentation using a powerful rich text editor. It's designed to be your project's central information hub.
whatIs.feature1: Rich text editing with formatting options (bold, italic, underline, lists, etc.)
whatIs.feature3: Project objectives, goals, and key information storage
whatIs.feature4: Team communication and project briefing space

projectTasks.title: Project Tasks
projectTasks.introduction: Project Tasks allow you to create, manage, and track individual work items within your projects. With proper permissions, you can access all the same functionalities as the main task management system, including creating, editing, deleting, detailed views, timesheet tracking, activities, and subtasks.

whatAre.title: What are Project Tasks?
whatAre.description: Project Tasks are individual work items that belong to a specific project. They provide the same comprehensive task management capabilities as the main task system but are organized within the project context for better project tracking and management.
whatAre.feature1: Create tasks directly within project context
whatAre.feature2: Full task editing and updating capabilities
whatAre.feature4: Detailed task views with comprehensive information
whatAre.feature5: Timesheet tracking, activities, and subtask management

permissions.title: Permission Requirements
permissions.description: To create and manage project tasks, you need proper task creation permissions. Users with project task permissions can access all task management functionalities within the project scope.

access.title: How to Access Project Tasks
access.description: To access and manage project tasks, follow these steps:
access.step1: Navigate to your Projects module from the main dashboard
access.step2: Click on any project from your project list to open project details
access.step3: Look for the 'Tasks' tab in the project details navigation bar
access.step4: Click on the Tasks tab to access the project task management interface

create.title: Create Tasks
create.item1: Add new tasks directly within the project context
create.item2: Set task details, assignees, priorities, and deadlines
create.item3: Configure task sources, types, and categories

edit.item1: Modify task information, descriptions, and settings
edit.item2: Update task status, priority, and assignment
edit.item3: Change task dates, deadlines, and progress tracking

details.item1: Access comprehensive task information and history
details.item2: View task comments, attachments, and activities
details.item3: Track task progress and completion status

interface.item3: Optimized layout for portrait and landscape modes
interface.item4: Large touch targets for easy mobile interaction

taskCategory.title: Task Category
taskCategory.introduction: Task Category is a classification system that helps you organize and categorize your tasks based on their type, purpose, or nature. You can select from existing categories or create new ones through task settings to better organize your workflow.

whatIs.title: What is Task Category?
whatIs.description: Task Category is a organizational feature that allows you to classify tasks under specific categories. This helps in better task management, filtering, reporting, and workflow organization by grouping similar types of tasks together.
whatIs.feature1: Organize tasks by type, purpose, or department
whatIs.feature2: Filter and search tasks based on categories
whatIs.feature4: Improve workflow organization and task visibility
whatIs.feature5: Create custom categories to match your business needs

select.title: How to Select Task Category
select.description: When creating or editing a task, you can select the appropriate category from the available options:
select.step1: Open the task creation or editing form
select.step2: Look for the 'Task Category' dropdown field
select.step3: Click on the dropdown to view available categories
select.step4: Select the most appropriate category for your task

create.title: How to Create New Task Categories
create.description: You can create custom task categories through the task settings to match your specific organizational needs:
create.step1: Navigate to the Tasks module from your dashboard
create.step2: Access Task Settings (usually found in the module toolbar or menu)
create.step3: Look for 'Categories' or 'Task Categories' section in settings
create.step4: Click 'Add New Category' or similar button
create.step5: Enter category name, description, and save your new category

settings.title: Task Settings Access
settings.description: Task categories are managed through Task Settings. You need appropriate permissions to access task settings and create new categories. Contact your administrator if you don't have access to task settings.

default.title: Default Categories
default.item2: Common categories like General, Urgent, Review, etc.
default.item3: Default categories can be used immediately

custom.title: Custom Categories
custom.item1: Create categories specific to your business needs
custom.item2: Edit existing category names and descriptions

platform.title: Web Platform Features

mobile.title: Mobile Task Categories

mobile.imageCaption: Mobile add task form with category selection dropdown showing available task categories

selection.title: How to Select Task Category
selection.description: When creating project tasks on mobile, you can select from available task categories to organize your work.

process.step1: Open the 'Add Task' form within a project
process.step3: Tap on the dropdown to view available categories
process.step4: Select the appropriate category for your task
process.step5: Complete the rest of the task details and save

limitations.title: Mobile Limitations
limitations.description: Mobile users cannot create new task categories. You can only select from existing categories that are available in the system.

milestone.title: Milestone
milestone.introduction: Milestones are important checkpoints in your project that help track progress and mark significant achievements. You can create milestones with specific dates and add relevant tasks to organize your project timeline effectively.

whatAre.title: What are Milestones?
whatAre.description: Milestones are significant points or events in a project timeline that represent important achievements, deadlines, or phases. They help break down projects into manageable segments and provide clear progress indicators.
whatAre.feature1: Mark important project phases and achievements
whatAre.feature2: Track project progress with clear checkpoints
whatAre.feature3: Organize tasks under specific milestone goals
whatAre.feature4: Set target dates for project deliverables
whatAre.feature5: Monitor team performance and project timeline

addMilestone.title: How to Add a Milestone
addMilestone.description: Creating a new milestone helps organize your project into manageable phases. Follow these steps to add a milestone:
addMilestone.step1: Navigate to your project details page
addMilestone.step2: Click on the 'Milestone' tab in the project navigation
addMilestone.step3: Click the 'Add Milestone' button (usually located in the top-right)
addMilestone.step4: Fill in the milestone details: Name, Description, Start Date, and End Date

addTasks.title: How to Add Tasks to Milestone
addTasks.description: Once you have created a milestone, you can add existing project tasks to it for better organization and tracking:
addTasks.step1: Go to the milestone you want to add tasks to
addTasks.step2: Click the 'Add Tasks to Milestone' button
addTasks.step3: Use the search function to find specific tasks if needed
addTasks.step4: Select the tasks you want to include by checking the boxes
addTasks.step5: Click 'Add' to assign the selected tasks to the milestone

deleteTasks.title: How to Delete Tasks from Milestone
deleteTasks.description: You can remove tasks from milestones when they are no longer relevant or need to be reassigned. This helps keep your milestones organized and accurate.
deleteTasks.step1: Navigate to the milestone containing the task you want to remove
deleteTasks.step2: Locate the task in the milestone task list
deleteTasks.step3: Click on the action button (three dots) next to the task
deleteTasks.step4: Select 'Delete task from milestone' from the dropdown menu

deleteMilestone.title: How to Delete Milestone
deleteMilestone.description: When a milestone is no longer needed or was created by mistake, you can delete it completely from the project. This action will remove the milestone and unassign all associated tasks.
deleteMilestone.step1: Go to the milestone you want to delete
deleteMilestone.step2: Click on the action button (three dots) for the milestone
deleteMilestone.step4: Confirm the deletion in the confirmation dialog
deleteMilestone.step5: Click 'Delete' to permanently remove the milestone

creation.title: Milestone Creation
creation.item1: Create milestones with custom names and descriptions
creation.item2: Set specific start and end dates for timeline tracking
creation.item3: Edit milestone details after creation if needed

tasks.title: Task Management
tasks.item1: Add multiple tasks to a single milestone
tasks.item2: Remove tasks from milestones when needed
tasks.item3: View all milestone tasks in organized lists

access.title: Access Requirements
access.description: Creating and managing milestones requires appropriate project permissions. Users need project management access to create milestones and add tasks. Contact your administrator if you don't have the necessary permissions.

platform.title: Web Platform Features
platform.description: Milestone management is fully available on the web platform with complete creation, editing, and task assignment capabilities. You can manage all aspects of milestones through the project interface.

mobile.title: Mobile Platform Limitation
mobile.description: Milestones are not available on the mobile platform. This feature is exclusively available on the web version of NovoTak.
mobile.webOnly: To create, manage, and view milestones, please use the web platform where full milestone functionality is available.

filesDocuments.title: Files & Documents
filesDocuments.introduction: NovoTak projects include dedicated Files and Documents tabs for comprehensive file management. Each tab serves different purposes and provides specific features for organizing and managing project-related content.

filesTab.title: Files Tab Overview
filesTab.description: The Files tab provides folder-based organization for all project files with comprehensive management capabilities:
filesTab.feature1: Create and manage folders for organized file structure
filesTab.feature2: Upload multiple files with drag-and-drop functionality
filesTab.feature3: View file details including size, type, and modification date
filesTab.feature4: Search and filter files by name, type, or date
filesTab.feature5: Download, share, and manage file permissions

documentsTab.title: Documents Tab Overview
documentsTab.description: The Documents tab focuses on document-specific management with advanced features for document workflows:
documentsTab.feature1: Add new documents with metadata and categorization
documentsTab.feature2: Sort documents by latest, name, or custom criteria
documentsTab.feature3: Advanced search functionality for document discovery

files.title: Files Tab
files.item4: Suitable for all file types and formats

documents.title: Documents Tab
documents.item4: Optimized for business documents and reports

detailedDocs.documentManagement: Document Management - Detailed documentation workflows, versioning, and approval processes

platform.description: Both Files and Documents tabs are fully available on the web platform with complete upload, organization, and management capabilities. Access these tabs from any project details page.

missionManagement.title: Mission Management
missionManagement.description: Missions are short-term, time-bound tasks with specific objectives and deadlines. Unlike projects, missions are designed for urgent, focused work that requires immediate attention and quick completion.

overview.title: Mission Overview
overview.description: The Mission tab provides a dedicated interface for managing time-sensitive tasks and urgent assignments. Missions have shorter durations compared to projects and are ideal for critical tasks that need immediate attention.
overview.imageAlt: Mission management interface showing mission list with time tracking and status indicators

differences.title: Mission vs Project Differences
differences.description: Understanding the key differences between missions and projects:
differences.duration: Duration: Missions are short-term (hours to days), Projects are long-term (weeks to months)
differences.timeframe: Time Tracking: Missions have precise start/end times, Projects have broader date ranges
differences.urgency: Priority: Missions are for urgent tasks, Projects are for planned initiatives
differences.scope: Scope: Missions have focused objectives, Projects have comprehensive deliverables
differences.tracking: Monitoring: Missions require real-time tracking, Projects use milestone-based progress

creation.title: Creating a New Mission
creation.description: Follow these steps to create a new mission with specific time constraints and objectives:
creation.step1: Navigate to Projects module and select the 'Mission' tab
creation.step2: Click the 'Add Mission' button to open the mission creation form
creation.step3: Fill in mission details: Name, Department, Start/End times, Objectives
creation.step4: Assign team members and set priority level (High, Medium, Low)
creation.step5: Save the mission and notify assigned team members

features.title: Mission Management Features
features.description: Key features available for mission management:
features.timeTracking: Real-time tracking with precise start and end times
features.statusUpdates: Live status updates (Not Started, In Progress, Completed, Overdue)
features.teamAssignment: Quick team member assignment with instant notifications
features.progressMonitoring: Real-time progress monitoring and completion tracking
features.reporting: Mission completion reports and time analysis

overview.title: Mobile Mission Management
overview.description: Access and manage missions on the go with the mobile app. View mission details, update status, and track progress from anywhere.
overview.imageAlt: Mobile mission interface showing mission list and detail view with time tracking

features.title: Mobile Mission Features
features.description: What you can do with missions on mobile:
features.viewMissions: View complete mission list with search and filter options
features.updateStatus: Update mission status and progress in real-time
features.timeTracking: Track time spent on missions with start/stop functionality
features.teamView: View assigned team members and their roles
features.notifications: Receive push notifications for mission updates and deadlines

limitations.title: Mobile Limitations
limitations.description: Mission creation and advanced configuration features are available on the web version only. Mobile users can view, update status, and track time but cannot create new missions or modify mission settings.

projectActions.title: Project Actions
projectActions.description: Manage your projects with various actions including editing, archiving, and status changes. Access these options through the three-dot menu on each project row.

actionsMenu.title: Project Actions Menu
actionsMenu.description: Access project actions through the three-dot menu in the project list. Each project row has an actions menu with the following options:
actionsMenu.editDesc: Modify project details, members, and settings
actionsMenu.archiveDesc: Move completed or inactive projects to archive without deleting them. Archived projects can be viewed separately and reactivated anytime. See the Archive section in the menu for more details.
actionsMenu.changeStatusDesc: Update project status to reflect current progress

edit.title: Edit Project
edit.description: Modify existing project details, update team members, and adjust project settings through the project actions menu.

editProject.title: Edit Project
editProject.description: Modify existing project details, update team members, and adjust project settings. Changes are saved automatically when you update the information.

steps.title: How to Edit a Project
steps.step1: Locate the project in your project list
steps.step2: Click the three-dot menu (⋮) on the project row
steps.step4: The project form will open with current information pre-filled
steps.step5: Update any fields you want to change:

archiveProject.title: Archive Project
archiveProject.description: Archive completed or inactive projects to keep your active project list organized. Archived projects are moved to a separate view but remain accessible.

steps.title: How to Archive a Project
steps.step1: Find the project you want to archive in the project list
steps.step2: Click the three-dot menu (⋮) on the project row
steps.step4: Confirm the archiving action in the dialog box
steps.step5: The project will be moved to the archived projects list

benefits.title: Benefits of Archiving
benefits.benefit1: Keeps active project list clean and focused
benefits.benefit3: Projects can be reactivated anytime if needed
benefits.benefit4: Maintains team access to archived project information

archivedProjects.title: Viewing Archived Projects
archivedProjects.description: Access and manage your archived projects through the dedicated archived projects view.

steps.title: How to View Archived Projects
steps.step1: Go to the Projects section from the main menu
steps.step2: Look for the "View Archived Projects" tab or button
steps.step3: Click to switch to the archived projects view
steps.step4: Browse through your archived projects list

actions.title: Available Actions for Archived Projects
actions.activateDesc: Move the project back to active status
actions.permanentDeleteDesc: Completely remove the project (use with caution)

activateProject.title: Activate Project
activateProject.description: Reactivate archived projects to bring them back to your active project list when work resumes.

steps.title: How to Activate an Archived Project
steps.step1: Navigate to the "View Archived Projects" section
steps.step2: Find the project you want to reactivate
steps.step3: Click the three-dot menu (⋮) on the archived project row
steps.step5: Confirm the activation in the dialog box
steps.step6: The project will return to your active projects list

workflow.title: Archive ↔ Activate Workflow
workflow.description: The archive and activate process is completely reversible:
workflow.archiveDesc: Active Project → Archived List (disappears from active view)
workflow.activateDesc: Archived Project → Active List (returns to active view)
workflow.switchingDesc: Use "View Archived Projects" and "View Active Projects" tabs to switch between lists

changeStatus.title: Change Project Status
changeStatus.description: Update your project status to reflect the current progress. Available statuses include Created, Started, Closed, Cancelled, InProgress, and Postpone.
changeStatus.createdDesc: Project has been created but not started
changeStatus.inProgressDesc: Project is actively being worked on

mobile.title: Mobile Project Actions
mobile.description: On mobile devices, project actions are available directly within the project detail view. You can edit, archive, and change project status without leaving the project interface.
mobile.imageAlt: Mobile project detail view showing available actions
mobile.imageCaption: Mobile project detail interface with edit, archive, and status change options

overview.title: Ticketing System Overview
overview.description: NovoTak's ticketing system provides a comprehensive solution for managing support requests, issues, and service tickets. Create ticketing projects with designated owners and members. Only team members who are NOT project owners or members can raise tickets inside projects. Project owners and members cannot create tickets but can assign tickets raised by others to resolvers. Ticket creators cannot be assigned to resolve their own tickets. All parties receive notifications when tickets are completed.

web.imageAlt: Ticketing system web interface showing ticket list with categories, assignments, and status tracking
web.dashboardImageAlt: Ticketing dashboard showing comprehensive ticket management interface with analytics and status overview

ticketOperations.feature2: Project owners/members assign tickets to resolvers
ticketOperations.feature3: Resolvers complete tasks and update status

viewOptions.feature2: Grid View for visual ticket overview

ticketingProjects.title: Ticketing Projects Management
ticketingProjects.description: Ticketing projects serve as containers for organizing related tickets and support requests. Each project has designated owners and members who can assign and manage tickets raised by non-members within that project scope.

creation.title: Creating Ticketing Projects
creation.description: Before tickets can be created, you must first establish a ticketing project. This project acts as a workspace where designated team members can manage support requests and issues.
creation.imageAlt: Ticketing project creation interface showing project setup form with owner and member assignment

permissionNotice.title: Permission & Role-Based Access:
permissionNotice.description: The ability to create ticketing projects depends on your user role and assigned permissions. Only users with appropriate project creation permissions can access this functionality. Contact your system administrator if you cannot create projects.

steps.title: Project Creation Steps:
steps.step1: Navigate to the Ticketing module and click 'Create New Project'
steps.step2: Fill in project details: name, description, department, and category
steps.step3: Assign project owners - these users will have full management rights
steps.step4: Add project members - these users can create and manage tickets within the project

step1.title: Navigate to Ticketing Module
step1.description: Go to the Ticketing module from the main navigation and click 'Create New Project' button to start creating a new ticketing project.

step2.title: Fill Basic Project Information
step2.description: Enter the project name and description. Note that SL No. (Serial Number) and Creator fields are automatically filled by the system.
step2.note: The SL No. and Creator fields are auto-generated and cannot be edited manually.

step3.title: Select Project Category
step3.description: Choose an appropriate category from the dropdown menu to classify your ticketing project type and organize it properly.

step4.title: Select Department
step4.description: Choose the department that will be responsible for managing this ticketing project from the department dropdown menu.
step4.imageAlt: Department dropdown selection interface showing available departments for ticketing project assignment

step5.title: Add Project Owners
step5.description: Click the 'Add Owners' button to select team members who will have full management rights over this ticketing project. Owners can assign tickets but cannot create them.
step5.imageAlt: Add project owners interface showing team member selection dialog for assigning ownership rights

step6.title: Add Project Members
step6.description: Click the 'Add Members' button to select team members who will participate in ticket management. Members can assign tickets but cannot create them within this project.
step6.imageAlt: Add project members interface showing team member selection dialog for project participation

step7.title: Save the Project
step7.description: Click the 'Save' button to create the ticketing project with all the specified settings, team assignments, and configurations.
step7.note: Once saved, the project will be available for ticket creation by non-owners and non-members.

process.step1: Initially, ticket is created with 'Not Started' status and awaiting assignment or action
process.step2: Access the ticket from your task list and click on the action menu (three dots)
process.step3: Select 'Change Status' from the action menu to open the status change form dialogue
process.step4: Choose the appropriate status according to your progress (In Progress, Completed, etc.)
process.step5: Save the status change to update the ticket progress

statusFlow.title: Status Flow & Visibility

statusFlow.inProgress: In Progress → Completed (when project owners and members complete the ticket)

statusFlow.visibility: Ticket creator can see the completed status but cannot change it themselves

addClient.title: Adding New Clients
addClient.description: Step-by-step guide to creating new client profiles in the system.

steps.step1: Navigate to the Clients section from the main dashboard
steps.step2: Click the 'Add Client' or '+' button to open a blank form with empty fields for client information
steps.step3: Fill in the required client information:

clientFormFields.title: Client Form Fields
clientFormFields.description: The client creation form uses a table-based layout with the following columns and field descriptions:

slNo.title: Sl No:
slNo.description: Auto-generated serial number for client identification and record keeping.

id.title: ID:
id.description: Unique client identifier code assigned to each new client.

creator.title: Creator:
creator.description: Non-editable field showing the team member who created this client record.

name.title: Name:
name.description: Click the icon in this field to open an "Upload Image" dialog where you can enter the client name and upload their image (Required).

clientNameField.title: Client Name Field:
clientNameField.description: Enter the client's full company name or individual name in the dialog.

addImage.title: Add Image:
addImage.description: Upload client logo or profile image (*.jpeg, *.jpg, *.png, *.gif formats, max 3 MB) for visual identification.

email.title: Email:
email.description: Primary contact email address for communication and notifications.

emirates.title: Emirates (Dropdown):
emirates.description: Select the appropriate Emirates location from the dropdown list for regional classification.

details.title: Details (Pop-up):
details.description: Click to open "Add Client Details" dialog for additional client information, contact details, and business specifications.

leads.title: Leads:
leads.description: This column will display lead information after leads are created and associated with this client.

sales.title: Sales:
sales.description: This column will show sales status after leads are converted to awarded or rejected status.

actions.title: Actions:
actions.description: Contains Save and Clear buttons for form submission and field clearing respectively.

finalSteps.title: Final Steps
finalSteps.step1: Review all entered information for accuracy
finalSteps.step2: Click 'Save' to create the client profile and add them to your database
finalSteps.step3: The new client will appear in your client list and be available for project assignment

deleteClient.title: How to Delete a Client
deleteClient.description: To delete a client from your database, follow these steps:
deleteClient.step1: Step 1: Click the three dots (⋮) in the Actions column for the client you want to delete
deleteClient.step2: Step 2: Select "Delete" from the dropdown menu options
deleteClient.step3: Step 3: A confirmation dialog will appear asking "Are you sure you want to delete?"
deleteClient.step4: Step 4: Click the red "Delete" button to confirm removal, or "Cancel" to abort the action
deleteClient.note: Note: This action permanently removes the client and cannot be undone. Ensure you have proper permissions before deleting.

accessingLeads.step1: Client Details View: Navigate to any client and click the 'Leads' tab to view and manage leads specific to that client
accessingLeads.step2: Client List View: From the main client list, click the 'Leads' tab to view all leads across all clients

creatingLeads.title: Creating Leads in Client Details
creatingLeads.description: To create a new lead within a specific client's details:
creatingLeads.step1: Navigate to the client details page by clicking on a client from the client list
creatingLeads.step2: Click on the 'Leads' tab in the client details interface
creatingLeads.step3: Fill in the lead information in the blank row at the top of the leads table
creatingLeads.step4: Enter Lead Name, Value (in AED), Source, Probability percentage, and Status
creatingLeads.step5: Click 'Save' to create the lead - it will appear in both Client Details > Leads and Client List > Leads views

interfaceOverview.actionsDesc: Edit and delete options for managing individual leads

managingLeads.rejectLeadDesc: Click the red 'Reject' button to mark the lead as lost/rejected

accessingTasks.title: Accessing Lead Tasks
accessingTasks.description: Navigate to any lead details page and click the 'Tasks' tab to access task management features. The interface provides the same functionality as the main task management module but is specifically filtered for the selected lead.
accessingTasks.imageCaption: Lead Details Tasks Interface - Access task management within lead details

creatingTasks.title: Creating Lead Tasks
creatingTasks.description: Task creation within lead details follows the same process as general task creation but automatically links tasks to the specific lead:
steps: • Step 1: Navigate to the lead details page and click the 'Tasks' tab
• Step 2: Use the blank field row at the top of the task list to input new task information
• Step 3: Enter the task description in the required Description field (Task ID and S.No. are auto-generated)
• Step 4: Select task source from dropdown options (General, Project, Mission, Client, Vendor)
• Step 5: Assign team members by clicking the Members field and selecting from the available team list
• Step 6: Set start and end dates using the date picker fields
• Step 7: Choose task priority and category from the respective dropdown menus
• Step 8: Click 'Save' to create the task - it will be automatically linked to the current lead

taskInterface.description: The lead task interface provides comprehensive task management capabilities:

features.description: Display tasks in list format with columns for S.No, Task ID, Source, Description, Members, Sub, Start Date, End Date, Type, Status, Priority, and Actions

features.title: View Options
features.description: Switch between Created, Assigned, and Backlog views using the status filter buttons

features.title: Search and Filter
features.description: Use the search bar to find specific tasks and filter by Status, Members, and Priorities using dropdown menus

features.title: Cost Tracking
features.description: Monitor task costs and duration with the Cost and Duration display in the top right

leadFiles.title: Lead Files Management
leadFiles.description: Manage files and attachments related to the specific lead through the Files tab:
steps: • Step 1: Navigate to the lead details page and click the 'Files' tab
• Step 2: Create folders to organize files by clicking the 'New Folder' option
• Step 3: Upload files by clicking 'Upload Files' or drag and drop files into the interface
• Step 4: Organize files into appropriate folders for easy access and management
• Step 5: Use the file actions menu to rename, move, or delete files as needed

leadDocuments.title: Lead Documents Management
leadDocuments.description: Store and manage important documents related to the lead through the Documents tab:
steps: • Step 1: Navigate to the lead details page and click the 'Documents' tab
• Step 2: Add new documents by clicking the 'Add Document' button
• Step 3: Fill in document details including title, description, and document type
• Step 4: Upload the document file (supported formats: PDF, DOC, DOCX, XLS, XLSX)
• Step 5: Set document permissions and access levels if required
• Step 6: Save the document to make it available for team members and future reference

addingContacts.title: Adding Client Contacts
addingContacts.description: Add new contacts to the client using the contacts interface:
steps: • Step 1: Navigate to the Client Details page and click on the 'Contacts' tab
• Step 2: Use the blank field row at the top to enter new contact information
• Step 3: Enter the contact name in the 'Client Name' field (this will be the contact person's name)
• Step 4: Add the contact's designation/job title in the 'Designation' field
• Step 5: Enter the contact's email address in the 'Email' field
• Step 6: Add the phone number using the phone number field with country code selection
• Step 7: Click 'Save' to add the contact to the client's contact list
• Step 8: Repeat the process to add multiple contacts for the same client
