addingGroups.title: Adding Sales Groups
addingGroups.description: To create a new sales group, click the 'Add Sales Group' button in the top-right corner of the interface. This will open a dialog where you can configure the group details and select team members.
addingGroups.dialogImageCaption: Add Sales Group dialog showing Group ID, Group Name fields and employee selection interface
steps: 1. Click Add Sales Group: Click the '+' button or 'Add Sales Group' button in the interface
2. Enter Group ID: Provide a unique identifier for the group (e.g., 'SG001', 'SALES_TEAM_A')
3. Enter Group Name: Give the group a descriptive name (e.g., 'Lead Generation Team', 'Enterprise Sales')
4. Search and Select Employees: Use the search field to find team members and select them using checkboxes
5. Review Selections: Verify the selected employees appear in the list with their details
6. Save Group: Click 'Add' to create the group or 'Cancel' to discard changes

fields.title: Group ID:
fields.description: Unique identifier for the sales group. Use alphanumeric characters and underscores (e.g., 'SG001', 'TEAM_NORTH')

fields.title: Group Name:
fields.description: Descriptive name for the group that clearly identifies its purpose or territory (e.g., 'Enterprise Sales Team', 'Regional North')

fields.title: Employee Search:
fields.description: Search field to find and filter employees by name, department, or designation for group assignment

fields.title: Employee Selection:
fields.description: Checkbox selection system to add multiple employees to the group. Shows employee details including ID, designation, and department

groupManagement.title: Group Management Interface
groupManagement.description: After creating groups, you can view and manage them through the main groups interface. The interface displays comprehensive information about each group member and provides management tools including edit and delete options in the top bar.
groupManagement.listImageCaption: Sales Groups list view showing group members with their status, departments, levels, and target information

topBarControls.title: Top Bar Management Controls:
topBarControls.editButton: Edit Group Button: Click the edit (pencil) icon in the top bar to modify group details, change group name, add or remove members
topBarControls.deleteButton: Delete Group Button: Click the delete (trash) icon in the top bar to remove the entire group (requires confirmation)
topBarControls.leadGroupLabel: Lead Group Label: Shows the current group name and provides context for management actions

editingGroups.title: Editing Sales Groups:
steps: 1. Select Group: Navigate to the group you want to edit
2. Click Edit Button: Click the pencil icon in the top bar
3. Modify Details: Update group name, ID, or member assignments
4. Add/Remove Members: Use employee search and selection to modify group membership
5. Save Changes: Confirm modifications to update the group

deletingGroups.title: Deleting Sales Groups:
steps: 1. Select Group: Navigate to the group you want to delete
2. Click Delete Button: Click the trash icon in the top bar
3. Confirm Deletion: System will ask for confirmation before removing the group
4. Data Protection: All group data and member assignments will be permanently removed

settingTargets.title: Setting Individual Member Targets
settingTargets.description: Sales groups support individual target setting for each member (not for the group as a whole). You can set monthly targets for individual team members to track their performance and monitor goal achievement throughout the year.
settingTargets.targetImageCaption: Edit Target interface showing monthly target setting for individual group members with year selection and monthly breakdown
settingTargets.importantNote: Important: Targets are set for individual members within the group, not for the entire group. Each member can have personalized monthly targets based on their role and capacity.
steps: 1. Access Target Setting: Click on the target icon or button for a specific group member
2. Select Year: Choose the target year from the dropdown (e.g., 2025)
3. Set Monthly Targets: Enter target values for each month (Jan through Dec)
4. Review Targets: Verify all monthly targets are set correctly
5. Save Changes: Click save to apply the targets to the group member

features.title: Annual Planning:
features.description: Set targets for entire calendar year with month-by-month breakdown for detailed planning

features.title: Individual Targets:
features.description: Each group member can have personalized targets based on their role and experience level

features.title: Monthly Tracking:
features.description: Monitor progress against monthly targets to identify trends and performance patterns

features.title: Performance Analytics:
features.description: Track achievement rates and compare actual performance against set targets

features.title: Target Adjustments:
features.description: Modify targets throughout the year based on market conditions and business needs

groupManagementInterface.title: Group Management Interface
groupManagementInterface.description: The group management interface provides comprehensive controls for editing and deleting sales groups. Users with appropriate permissions can manage groups using the top bar controls.
groupManagementInterface.imageCaption: Group Management Interface - showing edit and delete buttons for managing sales groups with Lead Group example
groupManagementInterface.functionality: Users can edit and delete groups by pressing the edit and delete buttons located in the top bar of the group interface. The edit button allows modification of group details, while the delete button removes the group entirely. These controls are only available to users with proper group management permissions.

memberDetailsView.title: Sales Member Details View
memberDetailsView.description: Access detailed information about individual sales group members including their performance metrics, targets, and achievements. This comprehensive view helps managers track individual performance within sales groups.
memberDetailsView.imageCaption: Sales Member Details View - showing individual member profile with performance metrics, targets achieved, and detailed analytics including charts and statistics
memberDetailsView.functionality: The member details view displays comprehensive information including member profile (name, role, contact details), performance metrics (targets achieved vs set), sales statistics (number of leads, conversion rates), achievement tracking with visual indicators, and performance analytics with charts showing trends over time. This view helps managers assess individual performance and provide targeted support.

mobileInterface.title: Mobile Sales Groups Interface
mobileInterface.description: On mobile, you can create and manage sales groups with full functionality through the Groups tab:

accessSteps.title: Accessing Sales Groups on Mobile
accessSteps.step1: Navigate to the Clients tab in the bottom navigation
accessSteps.step2: Tap on the 'Groups' tab to access sales groups management
accessSteps.step3: Use the plus (+) button to create new sales groups

creationProcess.title: Creating Sales Groups on Mobile
creationProcess.groupId: Enter Group ID: Provide a unique identifier for the group
creationProcess.groupName: Enter Group Name: Give the group a descriptive name
creationProcess.addMembers: Add Members: Use the 'Add Member' button to select team members for the group

limitations.title: Mobile Functionality
limitations.description: Mobile version provides full sales group creation and basic management. Advanced features like detailed target setting and performance analytics are optimized for web interface.

clientSettings.title: Client Settings Configuration
clientSettings.description: Configure and customize various aspects of the client management system including lead sources, sales statuses, reject reasons, and user permissions. These settings help standardize your client management workflow and ensure consistent data entry across your organization.

leadSource.title: Lead Source Management
leadSource.description: Configure the sources from which leads are generated. Lead sources help track the effectiveness of different marketing channels and acquisition methods.
leadSource.imageAlt: Lead Source settings interface showing available sources
leadSource.imageCaption: Lead Source configuration - manage and customize lead generation sources including Advertisement, Referral, Events, Social Media, Website, and Other options
leadSource.functionality: Add, edit, or remove lead sources to match your business needs. Each source can be customized with bilingual names (English and Arabic) and can be assigned to leads during creation. Default sources include Advertisement, Referral, Events, Social Media, Website, and Other.

salesStatuses.title: Sales Status Management
salesStatuses.description: Define and manage the various stages of your sales pipeline. Sales statuses help track lead progression and conversion rates.
salesStatuses.imageAlt: Sales Status settings interface showing pipeline stages
salesStatuses.imageCaption: Sales Status configuration - manage pipeline stages including Pending, Qualified, Contacted, and New status options with bilingual support
salesStatuses.functionality: Create custom sales statuses to reflect your sales process. Each status can be configured with English and Arabic names, and leads can be moved through different statuses as they progress through your sales funnel. Default statuses include Pending, Qualified, Contacted, and New.

rejectReasons.title: Reject Reasons Configuration
rejectReasons.description: Set up standardized reasons for lead rejection to maintain consistent documentation and improve future lead qualification processes.
rejectReasons.imageCaption: Reject Reasons configuration - define standardized rejection reasons for better lead management and reporting
rejectReasons.functionality: Define specific reasons why leads might be rejected, helping your team maintain consistent documentation. This data can be used for analysis to improve lead qualification processes and identify common rejection patterns.

createGroupPermissions.title: Create Group Permissions
createGroupPermissions.description: Manage user permissions for creating and managing sales groups. Control which team members can create new groups and assign employees to groups.
createGroupPermissions.imageAlt: Create Group Permissions interface showing user assignments
createGroupPermissions.imageCaption: Create Group Permissions - assign team members who can create and manage sales groups with employee selection interface
createGroupPermissions.functionality: Select specific team members who have permission to create sales groups. This includes the ability to create new groups, assign employees to groups, and manage group settings. Use the 'Add Employees' button to grant these permissions to selected team members.

viewAllClientsPermission.title: View All Clients Permission
viewAllClientsPermission.description: Control which users can view all client information across the organization versus only their assigned clients.
viewAllClientsPermission.imageAlt: View All Clients Permission interface showing user access control
viewAllClientsPermission.imageCaption: View All Clients Permission - manage user access to view all client information with team member selection and permission assignment
viewAllClientsPermission.functionality: Assign permissions to specific team members to view all clients in the system. Users without this permission can only see clients assigned to them or their groups. This helps maintain data security while allowing managers and supervisors broader access when needed.

mobileSection.title: Mobile Client Settings
mobileSection.description: Client settings configuration is primarily available on the web interface. Mobile users can utilize the configured settings when creating or updating clients and leads.
mobileSection.functionality: On mobile, users can select from pre-configured lead sources, sales statuses, and reject reasons when working with clients and leads. However, creating new settings or modifying existing ones requires web access with appropriate permissions.
mobileSection.webOnlyNote: Client settings are not available on mobile. All client settings configuration (Lead Sources, Sales Statuses, Reject Reasons, Permissions) must be done through the web interface. Mobile users can only use the pre-configured settings.
mobileSection.imageAlt: Mobile client settings interface showing available options
mobileSection.imageCaption: Mobile Client Settings - view and utilize configured settings for lead sources, sales statuses, and other client management options

overview.title: Vendor Management Module Overview
overview.description: The Vendor Management Module in NovoTak helps organizations streamline vendor onboarding, contract management, and task tracking. It provides a secure, centralized system for managing all vendor-related information, documents, and contracts — ensuring efficiency, compliance, and collaboration across teams.

keyFeatures.title: Key Features
features: Centralized vendor database
Vendor onboarding & profile management
Contract creation and tracking
Document management
Automated alerts & notifications
Role-based access control
Performance tracking and insights

gettingStarted.title: Getting Started
gettingStarted.description: From the main NovoTak dashboard, navigate to Vendors in the main menu. You'll see two available views:
views: List View – Displays vendors in a table format
Kanban View – Displays vendors as cards organized by status or category
filters: All Vendors – Shows all vendors in the system
My Vendors – Displays vendors created or assigned to you
Members Filter – Filter vendors based on specific team members

vendorDetails.title: Vendor Details
vendorDetails.description: When you click on a specific vendor, you'll navigate to the vendor details page which contains comprehensive information and management of contracts, tasks, and files.

vendorSettings.title: Vendor Settings
vendorSettings.description: Vendor settings allow you to customize contract types, contract statuses, and manage permissions. They can be accessed from the main vendors menu.

permissionNotice.title: Permission Notice:
permissionNotice.description: Settings icons (edit and delete) are only visible to users who have vendor settings management permissions. Users without permissions can only view current settings.

contractTypes.title: Contract Types
contractTypes.description: Manage different contract types with Arabic translations.

contractStatus.description: Customize contract statuses to track execution phases.

settingsAccess.description: Access to vendor settings requires special permissions.

vendorManagement.description: Learn how to add, edit, and manage vendor profiles in NovoTak. This section covers the complete vendor management workflow from creation to maintenance.

addVendor.title: How to Add Vendor
addVendor.description: Adding a new vendor to your system involves filling out a comprehensive form with vendor details. Follow the steps below to create a vendor profile.

fields.label: SL No:
fields.description: Auto-generated serial number by the system

fields.label: ID:
fields.description: Manual unique identifier for the vendor

fields.label: Name:
fields.description: Vendor name field with image upload option for vendor logo

fields.label: Details:
fields.description: Click 'Add Vendor Details' to open dialog for address, website, phone number, and location

fields.label: Contracts:
fields.description: Contract fields will display when creating contracts inside vendor details

editVendor.title: How to Edit Vendor
editVendor.description: To edit an existing vendor, locate the vendor in the vendor list and use the edit functionality. Only the vendor creator has permission to edit vendor information.
steps: • Locate the vendor in the vendor list
• Click on the vendor row or edit icon
• Modify the required fields in the vendor form
• Update vendor details, contact information, or other fields as needed
• Click 'Save' to confirm changes
• The updated information will be reflected in the vendor list

permissionNotice.title: Edit Permission:
permissionNotice.description: Only the user who created the vendor can edit vendor information. Other users can view vendor details but cannot make changes.

deleteVendor.title: How to Delete Vendor
deleteVendor.description: To delete a vendor, use the delete button in the vendor list. This action requires confirmation and can only be performed by the vendor creator.
steps: • Locate the vendor in the vendor list
• Click the delete button or delete icon
• Confirm the deletion when prompted
• The vendor will be permanently removed from the system

permissionNotice.title: Delete Permission:
permissionNotice.description: Only the user who created the vendor can delete vendor entries. This action is permanent and cannot be undone.

vendorManagement.mobileAlert: Vendor management features are limited on mobile. Full vendor creation and editing capabilities are available on the web version only.

vendorContacts.description: Learn how to manage vendor details, contacts, documents, and tasks within the vendor profile. This section covers comprehensive vendor information management and interaction tracking.

vendorDetails.title: Vendor Details Overview
vendorDetails.description: When you click on a specific vendor from the vendor list, you'll navigate to the vendor details page which contains comprehensive information and management tools for contracts, tasks, documents, and contact persons.

tabsOverview.title: Vendor Details Tabs
tabsOverview.description: The vendor details page is organized into several tabs, each providing specific functionality for managing different aspects of vendor information:
tabs: • Contracts Tab - View and manage vendor contracts (covered in Contract Management section)
• Documents Tab - Upload and organize vendor-related files and documents
• Tasks Tab - Create and track tasks related to vendor activities
• Files Tab - Additional file storage and organization
• Contacts Tab - Manage vendor contact persons and communication details

contactsTab.title: Contacts Tab
contactsTab.description: The Contacts tab allows you to manage vendor contact persons, their roles, and communication preferences. Add multiple contacts for different departments or functions within the vendor organization.
features: • Add multiple contact persons for the vendor
• Assign roles and responsibilities to each contact
• Manage contact information (phone, email, position)
• Set primary and secondary contacts
• Track communication history with contacts
• Update contact preferences and availability

documentsTab.title: Documents Tab
documentsTab.description: The Documents tab provides file management capabilities for vendor-related documents including contracts, certificates, compliance documents, and other important files.
features: • Upload various file types (PDF, Word, Excel, images)
• Create organized folder structure for documents
• Share documents with team members
• Version control for document updates
• Document expiry tracking and alerts
• Access permission management for sensitive files

tasksTab.title: Tasks Tab
tasksTab.description: The Tasks tab enables you to create and manage tasks related to vendor activities, track progress, and ensure timely completion of vendor-related work.
features: • Create vendor-specific tasks and assignments
• Assign tasks to team members
• Set deadlines and priority levels
• Track task progress and completion status
• Add task comments and progress updates
• Generate task reports and analytics

vendorContacts.contractReference: For detailed information about creating and managing vendor contracts, please refer to the Contract Management section in the next menu.
vendorContacts.mobileAlert: Vendor details and contact management features are available on mobile with limited functionality. Full document upload and advanced management features are available on the web version.

vendorDetailOverview.description: When you click on a specific vendor from the vendor list, you will navigate to the vendor details page. This interface provides a comprehensive overview of all vendor-related information organized in tabs for easy navigation and management.

interfaceOverview.title: Vendor Details Interface
interfaceOverview.description: The vendor details page is organized into several tabs, each providing specific functionality for managing different aspects of vendor information:
features: • Contracts tab - View and manage all vendor contracts with status tracking
• Documents tab - Upload, organize, and manage vendor-related files
• Tasks tab - Create and track tasks related to vendor activities
• Files tab - Additional file storage and organization capabilities
• Contacts tab - Manage vendor contact persons and communication details

contractsTab.title: Contracts Tab
contractsTab.description: The Contracts tab displays all contracts associated with the vendor in a comprehensive table format. You can view contract details, track status, and manage contract information.
features: • View all vendor contracts in table format
• Track contract status (Pending, Approved, Completed)
• Monitor contract values and delivery dates
• Access contract details and documentation
• Edit and update contract information
• Create new contracts for the vendor

documentsTab.title: Documents Tab
documentsTab.description: The Documents tab provides comprehensive file management capabilities for all vendor-related documents including contracts, certificates, compliance documents, and other important files.
features: • Upload various file types (PDF, Word, Excel, images)
• Create organized folder structure for documents
• Version control for document updates
• Document expiry tracking and alerts
• Access permission management for sensitive files

tasksTab.title: Tasks Tab
tasksTab.description: The Tasks tab enables you to create and manage tasks specifically related to vendor activities, ensuring all vendor-related work is tracked and completed on time.
features: • Create vendor-specific tasks and assignments
• Assign tasks to team members
• Set deadlines and priority levels
• Track task progress and completion status
• Add task comments and progress updates
• Generate task reports and analytics

filesTab.title: Files Tab
filesTab.description: The Files tab provides additional file storage and organization capabilities, separate from the Documents tab, for managing various types of vendor-related files.
features: • Store additional vendor-related files
• Create nested folder structures
• Organize files by categories or projects
• Quick file search and filtering
• File sharing with team members
• Bulk file operations and management

contactsTab.title: Contacts Tab
contactsTab.description: The Contacts tab allows you to manage all vendor contact persons, their roles, and communication preferences for effective vendor relationship management.
features: • Add multiple contact persons for the vendor
• Assign roles and responsibilities to each contact
• Manage contact information (phone, email, position)
• Set primary and secondary contacts
• Track communication history with contacts
• Update contact preferences and availability

vendorDetailOverview.contractReference: For detailed information about creating and managing vendor contracts, please refer to the Contract Management section in the next menu.

contractManagement.description: Learn how to create and manage vendor contracts through the vendor details interface. This comprehensive guide covers contract creation, field management, editing, and deletion processes.

contractAccess.title: Accessing Contract Management
contractAccess.description: To manage vendor contracts, you need to navigate to the specific vendor's details page and access the Contracts tab.
steps: • Navigate to the Vendor Management module from the main dashboard
• Click on the specific vendor from the vendor list
• Select the 'Contracts' tab from the vendor details interface
• View existing contracts or click 'Add Contract' to create new ones

addContract.title: How to Add Contract
addContract.description: Creating a new contract involves filling out a comprehensive form with all contract details. Each field serves a specific purpose in contract management and tracking.

contractFields.title: Contract Form Fields
contractFields.description: The contract creation form contains the following fields that must be completed:
fields: • Sl No - Serial number (automatically generated by the system)
• Contract ID - Unique identifier for the contract (user-defined)
• Creator - Contract creator name (automatically filled, non-editable)
• Contract Name - Descriptive name for the contract
• Delivery Date - Expected completion or delivery date
• Value - Contract monetary value with currency selection (AED, SAR, USD, etc.)
• Contract Type - Dropdown selection of predefined contract types
• Status - Current contract status (Pending, Approved, In Progress, Completed)
• Actions - Save, Delete, or Clear Fields buttons for form management

contractActions.title: Contract Form Actions
contractActions.description: After filling out the contract form, you have several action options:
actions: • Save - Save the contract with all entered information
• Delete - Remove the contract entry (only for unsaved drafts)
• Clear Fields - Reset all form fields to empty state

contractDetails.title: Contract Details Page
contractDetails.description: After creating a contract, clicking on it opens the contract details page with comprehensive management options and additional tabs for enhanced functionality.

contractManagement.contractDetailsImageAlt: Contract Details Page Interface with Tasks, Documents, and Files Tabs

contractTabs.title: Contract Detail Tabs
contractTabs.description: The contract details page includes several tabs for comprehensive contract management:
tabs: • Tasks Tab - Create and manage tasks related to the contract
• Documents Tab - Upload and organize contract-related documents
• Files Tab - Additional file storage and management capabilities

editContract.title: How to Edit Contract
editContract.description: Existing contracts can be modified through the contract management interface.
steps: • Navigate to the vendor's Contracts tab
• Locate the contract you want to edit in the contracts table
• Click the edit icon (pencil) in the Actions column
• Modify the required fields in the contract form
• Click 'Save' to update the contract information
• Verify the changes are reflected in the contracts list

contractManagement.editContractImageAlt: Edit Contract Interface showing contract form with editable fields

deleteContract.title: How to Delete Contract
deleteContract.description: Contracts can be permanently removed from the system when no longer needed.
steps: • Navigate to the vendor's Contracts tab
• Locate the contract you want to delete in the contracts table
• Click the delete icon (trash) in the Actions column
• Confirm the deletion in the confirmation dialog
• The contract will be permanently removed from the system
deleteContract.warning: Contract deletion is permanent and cannot be undone. Ensure you have backed up any important contract information before proceeding with deletion.

contractManagement.tabsReference: For detailed information about managing tasks, documents, and files within contracts, please refer to the respective guides in the Tasks Management, Document Management, and File Management sections.

vendorSettings.description: Configure vendor management settings including contract types, status options, and permissions for comprehensive vendor management control.

contractTypes.title: Contract Types
contractTypes.description: Manage and configure different types of contracts available for vendor management. This section allows you to create, edit, and organize contract categories.
features: • Project - Contracts related to specific projects and deliverables
• Safety - Safety-related contracts and compliance agreements
• Maintenance - Ongoing maintenance and support contracts
• Add New Types - Create custom contract types as needed
• Edit Existing - Modify contract type names and descriptions
• Delete Types - Remove unused contract types from the system
contractTypes.permissionNotice: Contract type management requires vendor settings permissions. Users without these permissions can only view existing contract types.

contractStatus.title: Contract Status
contractStatus.description: Configure the available status options for contract management. These statuses help track the lifecycle of vendor contracts.
features: • Pending - Contracts awaiting approval or review
• Approved - Contracts that have been approved for execution
• In Progress - Active contracts currently being executed
• Completed - Finished contracts with all deliverables met
• Cancelled - Contracts that have been terminated or cancelled
• Custom Status - Create additional status options as needed
contractStatus.permissionNotice: Contract status management requires vendor settings permissions. Users without these permissions can only view existing status options.

vendorSettings.contractStatusImageAlt: Contract Status Configuration showing different status options

viewAllContracts.title: View All Contracts Permission
viewAllContracts.description: Configure permissions for viewing all vendor contracts across the system. This setting controls who can access comprehensive contract information.
features: • List View Display - Show contracts in organized list format (no grid layout)
• Permission Control - Manage who can view all contracts
• Department Access - Control access by department or role
• Contract Visibility - Set visibility levels for different user types
• Search and Filter - Enable advanced search capabilities
• Export Options - Allow contract data export for authorized users
viewAllContracts.permissionNotice: View All Contracts permission management requires administrative privileges. Only users with vendor settings management permissions can modify these access controls.

settingsIcons.title: Settings Icons and Permissions
settingsIcons.description: Settings icons (edit and delete) are only visible to users who have vendor settings management permissions. Users without permissions can only view current settings.
features: • Edit Icon - Visible only to users with vendor settings management permissions
• Delete Icon - Visible only to users with vendor settings management permissions
• Role-Based Visibility - Icons appear based on user's assigned role and permissions
• Permission Hierarchy - Different permission levels control different settings access
• View-Only Mode - Users without permissions see settings in read-only format
• Administrative Control - Super admins can manage all settings regardless of specific permissions
settingsIcons.permissionNotice: Settings icons (edit and delete) are only visible to users who have vendor settings management permissions. Users without permissions can only view current settings.

vendorSettings.viewAllContractsImageAlt: View All Contracts Permission interface showing list view without grid layout
vendorSettings.mobileAlert: Vendor management settings are only available on the web version. Mobile users can only view current settings.

organizationTools.title: File Manager Dashboard
organizationTools.feature1: • System Folder: Automatically created folders for projects, clients, vendors
organizationTools.feature2: • General Folder: Create custom files and folders for general use
organizationTools.feature3: • My Shares: Access shared folders, files, documents, and user content
organizationTools.feature4: • Cross-Module Access: Files from tasks, projects, clients, vendors, and chat

mobile.title: The NovoTak mobile app provides streamlined Files Management with essential functionality for accessing, organizing, and sharing files on the go.

features.title: Mobile Features
features.feature1: Access files from all modules in one centralized location
features.feature2: Create and organize files with folder structure
features.feature3: Share files with team members and control access
features.feature4: Filter and search files by date and other criteria

gettingStarted.title: Getting Started
gettingStarted.description: Access the Files menu from any module to view and manage your files. The mobile interface provides streamlined access to essential file operations and sharing features.

fileOperations.description: Learn how to create, manage, and organize files and folders in NovoTak. Access files from all modules or create general-purpose files directly in the File Manager.

upload.title: How to Create Files & Folders
upload.description: Access the Files Menu from any module to view all created files, or create new files directly in the File Manager dashboard.

supportedFormats.title: Member Selection Dialog
supportedFormats.description: When selecting members, you'll see options for: All Members, Share with Me, and Select Members for granular control over file access.

quickActions.title: Mobile File Operations
quickActions.description: Access files from any module through the Files menu. Create and manage files with simplified mobile interface optimized for touch interactions.

sharingDialog.title: Sharing Dialog & Access Control
sharingDialog.description: When sharing files and folders on mobile, access comprehensive sharing options through intuitive dialog interfaces designed for touch interaction.

fileFolderActions.title: File/Folder Actions
fileFolderActions.description: Tap the three dots (...) next to any file to access mobile-optimized actions. The options panel appears at the bottom with Edit, View members, Delete, and Cancel options for efficient file management.

folderManagement.description: Manage your files and folders with comprehensive actions including editing, sharing, and deletion. Control access and organize your digital workspace efficiently.

creation.title: File & Folder Actions
creation.description: After creating files or folders, you can perform various management actions to organize and control access to your content.

organizationTip.title: No Sharing Limits
organizationTip.description: There are no restrictions on the number of members you can share files with. You can share with individuals, groups, or the entire organization as needed.

navigation.title: Mobile File Management
navigation.description: Access essential file management actions through the mobile interface. Edit, share, and organize files with touch-optimized controls and simplified workflows.

sharingPermissions.description: Control file access with flexible sharing options and member selection. Manage permissions for individuals, groups, or organization-wide access with comprehensive security controls.

accessControl.title: Member Selection & Sharing Options
accessControl.description: When selecting members for file sharing, you have multiple options to control access levels and ensure appropriate permissions for your content.

securityNote.title: Security Best Practices
securityNote.description: Use "Select Members" for sensitive documents, "All Members" for general resources, and regularly review shared file permissions to maintain security compliance.

quickShare.title: Mobile Sharing Controls
quickShare.description: Access sharing options through the mobile interface with simplified member selection and permission controls optimized for mobile workflows.

searchFilter.description: Efficiently locate files and folders using advanced search and filtering capabilities. Use date selection and various filter criteria to quickly find the content you need.

advancedSearch.title: Search & Filter Options
advancedSearch.description: The File Manager provides comprehensive search and filtering tools to help you locate files across your entire digital workspace.

quickSearch.title: Mobile Search Features
quickSearch.description: Access essential search and filtering options through the mobile interface with touch-optimized controls for quick file discovery and organization.

features: Access files from all modules in one centralized location

advancedFiltering.description: Efficiently locate files and folders using advanced search and filtering capabilities

dateRangeSelection.title: Date Range Selection
dateRangeSelection.description: Select specific date ranges to view files created or modified during particular time periods

dashboardStructure.description: The File Manager dashboard is organized into two main categories that provide comprehensive file organization and management capabilities:

systemFolders.title: 1. System Folders
systemFolders.automaticallyCreated: Automatic Creation: System folders are automatically created when you add projects, clients, or vendors to your NovoTak system.
systemFolders.myShared: My Shared: Files shared with you by other team members
systemFolders.clients: Clients: Dedicated folders for each client with their associated documents
systemFolders.projects: Projects: Project-specific folders containing all related files and documents
systemFolders.additionalDescription: Vendors, Tasks, Personal, Chat, Users - each module automatically creates its dedicated folder structure for organized file management.

generalFolders.title: 2. General Folders
generalFolders.userCreated: User Creation: Custom folders you create for specific organizational needs beyond the system-generated structure.
features: Create custom folder hierarchies for departmental files
Organize templates, resources, and reference materials
Store company-wide documents and policies
Maintain archive folders for historical documents
Set up collaborative workspaces for cross-functional teams

infoAlert.title: Dual Folder Structure
infoAlert.description: This dual folder structure ensures system-generated content remains automatically organized while providing flexibility for custom organizational needs. System folders maintain data integrity and module relationships, while General folders offer unlimited customization for specific workflow requirements.

fileOperations.description: Learn how to create, manage, and organize files and folders in NovoTak. Access files from all modules or create files for general purposes directly in the File Manager.
fileOperations.accessDescription: Access the file list from any module to view all created files, or create new files directly in the File Manager dashboard.

systemFolder.title: 1. System Folder - Automatically created when adding projects, clients, or vendors
features: Contains organized folders for each project, client, and vendor
Includes My Shares with shared folders, files, documents, users, chat, tasks, projects, vendors, and clients
Features date selection and filtering options

generalFolder.title: 2. General Folder - Create custom files and folders for general use
generalFolder.description: Create general files using the file creation form

formFields.title: File Creation Form Fields:
formFields.creator: Creator: Auto-filled with current user
formFields.type: Type: Dropdown to choose folder or file option
formFields.name: Name: Enter folder or file name based on selection
formFields.addMembers: Add Members: Choose team members to share with
formFields.save: Save: Click to create the file/folder

memberSelectionAlert.title: Member Selection Dialog
memberSelectionAlert.description: When selecting members, you'll see options for: All Members, Share with me, and Select Members for granular file access control.

fileActionsMenu.title: File Actions Menu
fileActionsMenu.description: Once files and folders are created, you can manage them using the three-dot actions menu. Access comprehensive file management options including editing properties, sharing controls, and deletion capabilities.

edit.title: Edit - Modify file and folder properties
features: Update file/folder names and descriptions
Modify metadata and organizational tags
Change file categories and classifications

share.title: Share - Control file access and sharing permissions
features: Share with all team members for organization-wide access
Select specific members for granular access control
Manage sharing permissions and access levels
Track sharing history and audit file access

delete.title: Delete - Remove files and folders from the system
features: Permanently remove unwanted files and folders
Confirmation dialog prevents accidental deletions
Requires appropriate permissions for deletion actions

sharingDialog.title: Sharing Dialog & Access Control
sharingDialog.description: When you click "Share" from the actions menu, a comprehensive sharing dialog opens with multiple access control options to manage who can see your files and folders.
sharingDialog.visibilityControl: Files and folders shared with specific members are only visible to those selected users. Content that is not shared remains completely hidden from other team members, ensuring secure access control throughout your organization.

shareForAll.title: Share for All
shareForAll.subtitle: Organization-wide access
shareForAll.description: Make the file or folder accessible to all team members in your organization

shareWithMe.title: Share with me
shareWithMe.subtitle: Private access
shareWithMe.description: Keep the file or folder private to your account only

selectMembers.title: Select Members
selectMembers.subtitle: Controlled access
features: Choose specific team members from the searchable member list
View member details including ID, designation, department, and status
Only selected members can access the shared content

fileActionPermissions.title: File Action Permissions
fileActionPermissions.description: Understanding permission-based access to file operations. Different user roles have varying levels of access to file creation, editing, sharing, and deletion capabilities based on their assigned permissions within the organization.

quickActions.title: Mobile File Operations
quickActions.description: Access files from any module through the file list. Create and manage files with a simplified mobile interface optimized for touch interaction.

sharingDialog.title: Sharing Dialog & Access Control
sharingDialog.description: When sharing files and folders on mobile, access comprehensive sharing options through intuitive dialog interfaces designed for touch interaction.

fileFolderActions.title: File/Folder Actions
fileFolderActions.description: Tap the three dots (...) next to any file to access mobile-optimized actions. The options panel appears at the bottom with edit, view members, delete, and cancel options for efficient file management.

creatingFolders.title: 1. Creating New Folders
steps: Tap the "+" icon in the top-right corner of any folder view
Choose "Create Folder" from the options menu
Enter folder name and choose sharing options
Tap "Create" to save the new folder

uploadingFiles.title: 2. Uploading Files
steps: Tap the "+" icon and select "Upload File"
Choose files from your device gallery or camera
Files are automatically organized with timestamps

sharingOptions.title: 3. Sharing Options
sharingOptions.shareForAll: Share for All: Available to all team members
sharingOptions.shareToMe: Share to Me: Private files visible only to you
sharingOptions.selectMembers: Select Members: Choose specific team members for access

folderManagement.description: Manage your files and folders with comprehensive actions including editing, sharing, and deletion. Control access and organize your digital workspace efficiently.
folderManagement.webDescription: Create unlimited nested folder structures and manage comprehensive file organization with flexible sharing controls that determine content visibility.

fileAndFolderActions.title: File & Folder Actions
fileAndFolderActions.description: Create unlimited nested folder structures and manage comprehensive file organization with flexible sharing controls that determine content visibility.

unlimitedNested.title: Unlimited Nested Structure - Create folders inside folders endlessly
features: Create folders within existing folders without depth restrictions
Add files directly inside any folder at any level
Build complex organizational hierarchies to match workflow needs

sharingControls.title: Sharing Visibility Controls - Access is determined by sharing permissions
features: If a folder or file is not shared with a user, they cannot see it
Shared folders automatically grant access to all contents within them
Individual files can have different sharing settings than their parent folder

fileInfoPanel.title: File Information Panel
fileInfoPanel.description: When clicking on any file, a right-side panel opens displaying comprehensive file information and action options.

fileDetails.title: File Details Display - Complete file metadata
features: File name with full extension and unique identifier
File size displayed in appropriate units (KB, MB, GB)
Last modified date and time
File type identification (image, document, etc.)

actionButtons.title: Action Buttons - File interaction options
actionButtons.open: Open: View or edit the file in appropriate application
actionButtons.download: Download: Save the file to your local device

infoAlert.title: File Information Panel
infoAlert.description: The file information panel provides instant access to file details and actions without navigating away from your current folder view. This streamlines file management and improves workflow efficiency.

noSharingLimits.title: No Sharing Limits
noSharingLimits.description: There are no restrictions on how many members you can share files with. You can share with individuals, groups, or the entire organization as needed.

sharingPermissions.description: Control file access with flexible sharing options and member selection. Manage permissions for individuals, groups, or organization-wide access with comprehensive security controls.

allMembers.title: All Members
allMembers.features: Grants access to every member in your organization. Perfect for company-wide documents and policies. Automatically includes new team members.

shareWithMe.title: Share with Me
shareWithMe.features: View files that have been shared specifically with you. Access content from other team members. Central location for received files.

selectMembers.title: Select Members
selectMembers.features: Choose specific individuals or teams. Perfect for project-specific documents. Maintain confidentiality for sensitive files. No limit on number of selected members.

memberSelection.title: Member Selection and Sharing Options
memberSelection.description: When selecting members to share files with, you have multiple options to control access levels and ensure appropriate permissions for your content.

securityBestPractices.title: Security Best Practices
securityBestPractices.description: Use 'Select Members' for sensitive documents, 'All Members' for general resources, and regularly review shared file permissions to maintain security compliance.

dynamicPermissions.title: Dynamic Permissions
dynamicPermissions.description: Real-time permission updates with instant access control changes

unlimitedSharing.title: Unlimited Sharing
unlimitedSharing.description: No restrictions on the number of members you can share files with

crossModuleIntegration.title: Cross-Module Integration
crossModuleIntegration.description: Seamless file sharing across all NovoTak modules and departments

auditTrail.title: Audit Trail

mobileSharingControls.title: Mobile Sharing Controls
mobileSharingControls.description: Access sharing options through the mobile interface with simplified member selection and permission controls optimized for mobile workflows.

fileActionsMenu.title: File Actions Menu

searchAndFilterOptions.description: The File Manager provides comprehensive search and filtering tools to help you locate files across your entire digital workspace.

dateSelection.features: Select custom date ranges for search. Find files created or modified within specific periods. Track recent activity on files and folders.

locationBasedSearch.features: Scope search to specific folders. Search within project or client folders. Filter by file creator or owner.

sharingStatus.title: Sharing Status
sharingStatus.features: View only files shared with you. Find files you've shared with others. Identify private vs. public files.

features: Search terms match against all file attributes simultaneously
No need to specify search categories - system searches everything

searchTips.title: Search Tips
searchTips.description: The search function is global and comprehensive - simply enter any term related to your file (name, type, creator, etc.) and the system will search across all file attributes. Combine multiple filters for more precise results and use date ranges with file type filters to quickly locate specific documents from particular time periods.

Create button - Saves the folder with selected settings
Close button - Cancels folder creation and returns to main view
